Only the Chichken lollipop was good and rest are bad in taste. Ordered special biryani for Main course and the taste was not superb and the cost is bit high as per the taste. And the surprise thing is only they have soft drinks for desert and nothing else. Coming to the service, It was good. So i don't feel good to have dine out here.

Author: Raj Vardhan
Description: A good place to have Biryani. Taste quality and price completely matches. Don't have lolipop or desi or normal chicken Biryani but boneless Biryani is good one. Go and have once.

Author: Preeti Gupta
Description: Value for money.  Good to go when u have only food (to stuff in your stomach) on mind. It's tasty and hygienic (if they don't start brooming around while you are eating!). Most likely my experience of the simultaneous cleaning was a mere coincidence. By the way,  would suggest ordering boneless chicken items since I personally wasn't happy with the pieces they served in the chicken dish we ordered.

Author: Jayesh Gajre
Description:
We went there on a Sunday evening, precisely when all the bachelors of Bangalore are out in search of a good meal. Since we were early in the game and reached this place at around 7, getting a place to sit was not a problem. If you plan to have your dinner here, make it a point to arrive by 7:30. By 8, this place becomes very crowded and you have to spend at least 15 minutes waiting for a table.

On to the location, this place is located right on the Outer Ring road in Marathahalli, hence, finding this place isn't a problem. Finding a parking space for your vehicle may be, especially if it has four wheels. You can park your bike right on the other side of the service road, but parking a car can make you sweat. Fortunately, there aren't many buildings on the Bellandur side of Outer Ring road, so you can find less-crowded spaces to park on that side than on Marathahalli bridge side.

The ambiance of this place is decent. Both the floors are air conditioned and seating area is decent. On hygiene front, it looks pretty.

We ordered Apollo Fish as a starter and one boneless chicken biryani & a mutton biryani in main course. The starter hasn't got a taste that would make you remember it for a long time. The quantity is fine. If you look at it from the hunger perspective, the biryanis are sumptuous enough to make you consider giving starters a skip.

On to the biryanis, they are the USP of this place, and rightly so. The taste is really good! If you are a seasoned biryani lover and haven't checked this place out yet, you absolutely should.

Mutton Biryani had succulent pieces of meat that were well-cooked and marinated enough. Boneless chicken pieces were tasty too.

So where does this place miss out on a 4.5? Well, contamination and non-homogeneity of biryani rice. Allow me to explain.

Apparently, the chefs at Meghana were so happy with us that they awarded their own hair in the chicken biryani. That was a big turnoff. We were in no mood of fighting over it, though, as the waiter was extremely embarrassed and took away the whole biryani and offered a replacement. As we had already consumed most of it, we declined, and he didn't bill us on the chicken biryani.

On to the homogeneity of rice, you expect the spices of biryani to increase linearly as you tuck in deeper. However, a few lumps of spices were present somewhere in the middle with rice and with no meat. It leaves a weird taste in your mouth when a lump of a mixture of spices suddenly wrecks havoc! Gulps of water only partially soothes the hotness.

Overall, this place should be a little more vigilant on its process of preparation. However, as a die-hard biryani lover, I do recommend you to go this place. I am sure that the probability of what we faced is very low :)

Author: Akhil Subhedar
Description:
Went here with my colleagues for a team lunch on a Friday afternoon. The place was stuffed to the brim with people, though all in all it was a lovely experience. Something you must try if you like spicy food.
PARKING & ENTRY
- 3/5 - There is no specific parking for this joint, so you jusst have to find place on the service road. Not difficult, but slightly annoying. The place is above Sony world, right opposite Absolute Barbecue's on the Outer Ring Road.
  
RESERVATION
& SEATING - 3.5/5 - The seating arrangement is alright, comfortable but nothing special. There are ample tables available, but they have a huge crowd coming in for meals at all times, so a reservation is recommended. There is no place to wait, so if you go without reservation, you may end up standing on the road.

EXPERIENCE & AMBIANCE
- 2.5/5 - The waiters are not very friendly, nor do they seem interested in serving you. The ambiance is very mediocre. They serve the food in leaves, which I personally do not find comfortable to eat from. This is probably the only negative point about this place. 

FOOD
- 5/5 - Lovely food. We had the mutton biryani, the special chicken biryani and lemon garnished fish to start with. The Biryani was really good, though if you cannot take spicy food I'd suggest avoiding this place. Chicken biryani was spicier as compared to the mutton biryani. None of them lacked flavor though. The fish was well cooked and tasty. Something they could work upon is making the special biryani boneless. It would be a lot more comfortable to eat that way. They have several fish starters, and they serve normal food including but not limited to Roti and Gravy.

DESERTS
- N/A - Did not indulge.

EXPENSE
- 4.5/5 - The place is very well priced. We spent about Rs. 1900 in a group of 7 hardcore eaters. Each biryani, including tax etc. will cost you about Rs. 250. This is sufficient for 2 people if you are having other items to eat, but will suffice only 1 person if you are here eating only the biryani.

Author: Anu TJ
Description:
The place is biggggg! Since we went in a big group it could conveniently accommodate our lot! We took the Meghanas special boneless biryani! The quantity was tremendous. Two or more people can share one biryani. The boneless chicken was tender and spicy. We also ordered lime juice to go with the spicy biryanis. We ordered a baby corn starter and some two dishes in chicken. The starters tasted good too!
We were satisfied with what we ate.

Author: Mayuri Govil
Description: My most favorite place for having Biryani! When in doubt, go to Meghana :-) Love the chicken biryani, boneless, with bone, Megahan special and veg biryani. Good work guys, keep it up!

Author: Priyanshi Durbha
Description:
Frankly, I'm not such a fan of Meghana's. The biryanis are a tad too spicy and have a putting off food colouring. I tried their mutton biryani which left me craving for better. The meat wasn't of the best quality, and people streaming in all the time doesn't make things any better. The food doesn't seem very fresh.
My husband tried a paneer butter masala which was so excessively sweet that it didn't make sense for us to wait for a dessert. I'd go back to biryani zone which I found miles ahead of meghana's in all aspects.

The only star attraction for the marathalli branch is a pan shop right at the entrance, who makes delectable ice paans.

Author: Tharun Kilari
Description:
Hi Foodies!
  I am a Foodie and always search for best place to dine before settle down in a new city. I have tasted Biryani's all over the places where i kept my foot. Yesterday i went to Meghana Marthalli after a much hype and people recommending me to try this one as its the best one in City. I have tasted their biryani in Hyderabad and i found its good in terms of aroma but its not worth to compare it with Hyderabad Biryani. When i went in to this Restaurant i was welcome with Huge Crowd rather than the Aroma usally comes from a kitchen which serves biryani. There was Huge crowd which was managed by two people calling out their names as per the list given to them. We gave our details to one guy for the table and we got it in 20mins for afternoon lunch.
  So many told me try their Special Biryani as we were three so we order Chicken Biryani, Boneless Biryani and a Special Biryani. I really liked they serve on Banana leaf that was nice. I served myself the Special Biryani which comes with Boneless Chicken Pieces marinated in Egg and boiled which was ok. But the Rice was spicy and saltyyyyy.... I didnt get the punch that i usally get when i eat at SHADAB at Hyderabad. So i went for Chicken Boneless which also tasted the same with Boneless pieces spead on top Rice which was felt little sweet... Finally i tasted the Chicken Biryani and regreted for the previous two choices becoz this once was kinda of tasted like a Biryani and was balanced in spices and i felt ok. I was so disappointed about the experience that the Biryani was not up to my Expectations. I came to a Conclusion that i ate Biryani's from masters so i may kinda of not liked this versions of Biryani. The Bengularians who never tasted the real Hyderabadi Biryani particularly from Shadab-Hyderabad will defintely like this and they are regularly visitors. But for me It didnt reach my expectations and i dont call this a Biryani..... I  never tasted their starters because they taste well like other competitors only one thing on my mind was the Biryani ... So i can say i am bit disappointed ... And i Miss SHADAB, Hyderabad  a lot ...... Sorry Folks this is my frank opinion...

Author: Sainath Kumar
Description:
Very poor delivery and poor in customer handling . Good is not that great ..
If you order and Orders it takes hell lot of time
I order for a biryani and finally I got it with hair on it .. I feel its unhygienic

Author: Siddharth Mathi
Description: My friends had told me Meghana biryani is the best biryani place in Bangalore but somehow I had never eaten in Megahana. After almost a year i went with my office friends and regretted the fact that I had not eaten earlier here. The taste of the biryani is just out of this world. Also the quantity is so much that you would struggle to finish one. I never thought I would say this but there is a biryani better than nagarjuna's. Meghana's is awesome.

Author: Jegan
Description: We went for a late dinner and the place was still bustling. Parking is a hassle as its on a busy service road. So plan accordingly. The service was fast. We ordered the days special chicken dish, paneer tikka and meghana special biryani. The starters were really tasty and had ample portions. The combination of cashews and boneless chicken was awesome. We mainly went there for the biryani and was a tad disappointed. It was a strange mix and though the chicken in the biryani was delicious, the biryani rice was not to our liking. It was more hyderabadi style rice while I was expecting Andhra style. It's a very decent place to have good quality food with fast service.

Author: Swetalina Nayak
Description: Went with family. First thing I liked about this place is the use of banana leaves instead of plates. Ordered a wide variety of food. Chicken and prawn dishes as starter. Couple of biryani variety. Chicken curry n chappatis. Had a prompt service. Liked this place. Taste wise also its remarkable.

Author: Vamsi Krishna
Description: Best Andhra style biryani you can find in Bangalore. Quite a good place for lunch or dinner if looking for traditional south indian style. Been there countless times ?

Author: Sachin Pandit
Description:
Meghana foods is one stop shop for all your tastebud-needs as long as those needs route you to the South Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. They are not high on ambience or price, both being mediocre, give you a right option for Hop-in-gobble-hop-out kinda eatery. Staff has a mixture of courteous and "not-worthy-to-be-called-as-waiter"s so your WAITING experience will entirely depend on who gets to wait on you. My visits have seen a mixture of experiences from a courtesy perspective but the food has always had the better of me! Hence I keep revisiting this place. Do try the Paneer Biryani if you're a veggie. For omnivores, it doesn't really matter what you pick, coz be it seafood or two-wheelers(chicken) or four-wheelers(mutton) you're surely gonna be lickin your fingers clean(unless,of course you use a spoon ? ). If you're going between Fri-Sun, the best time to visit this place would be before 1PM cuz the service is gonna screw your happiness if the crowd is in full-swing

Update-2: last Saturday, we had ordered a delivery of biryanis for 10-12 people. It was WAY TOO SPICY.. While I like spicy food and was going bananas over it, the others at my office were gasping for breath. Not good guys.. Not good at all.

Bon appétit

Author: Sujit Reddy G
Description: Food and service in this branch is not as good as the one in Jayanagar or Koramangala. I was there on a Sunday afternoon, it was hot and none of the ACs were on, they need to clean this place and the food, especially when compared to food in other branches, it's not good.

Author: Happy Garg
Description: Tried it after hearing rave reviews from everywhere. Had few veg and chicken starters and bone less chicken biryani. The overall composition and taste was far from good. They din't even serve dahi and salan initially only after saying multiple times they did. Cold drinks were given in dirty bottles. Leave all this, if only the taste of biryani was good. Never return back to this place nor recommend to anyone. Even small time dhabas and eateries make better tasting biryani.

Author: Aditya
Description:
If you are a biryani lover.. Then Meghana food is the one stop place u can go..
Their boneless chilli chicken biryani is the best in market.. But u need to have appetite for some spicy stuff as it really makes u sweat..
I didn't feel like trying anything else at meghanas food.. It's always same biryani with thums up and it never goes wrong and I never get tired of trying it..
Worth a visit

Author: Neethu
Description: I have been to meghana foods twice. Starters are very spicy and tasty.So I expected biryani in the same way but disappointed. It is good compared to other biryani centres in marathahalli.

Author: Ramya Yerra
Description: Best biryani in Bangalore!! Amazing taste. Boneless Chicken Biryani is a must try. The aroma, spice, flavor...  Unbeatable!!  Lemon Chicken and chilly egg served here are delicious!!  So yummy!!

Author: MANU JOY
Description:
food is good. 

Just that they discriminate a lot when it comes to delivery. 

"we will delivery on this side of the road but not that side of the road"

Author: Hamar
Description:
My all time best place to dine in Bangalore is none other than Meghana with out a doubt! Same taste, spice, quality and quantity! Loving it!
Major concern is that the prices(Chicken Biryani 180, Meghana Biryani 190) are increased way too high for a regular and economic customers to buy frequently and the major drawback I cud see is Car Parking as well.

Author: Avinash Anand
Description:
'Lovely Ambience' is the exact word that come to my mind when i entered the place. This is a family restaurant. A truly lovely place for your family to have good food. The place is very well lighted. Service is great. The waiters are really good and friendly. Food is tasty. The only thing which i thought that i reduce my rating for, is they serve food on banana tree leaf. They do no use plates up there. But the food makes up for this. We went for a dinner and orderd meghana special biriyani. Its was good. Quantity was fine. Good enough for one person. The price is on the expensive side but food and ambience makes up for it.
Overall, i would suggest to go there and try out.
Ambience 4.5/5
Food 3.5/5
Service 4/5

Author: J Vallabha
Description: Had the day's special... Malabar chicken, with the special chicken biriyani... Malabar chicken was really very good... Highly recommended if it's the day's special...

Author: Abhijeet Samarthya
Description: My roommate and friend likes a lot biriyani from meghna so we end up ordering often from here. A different way but typical andhra style,place is crowded so better do home delivery..starters and meghna special biriyani are better than good

Author: Santhan Raghavendra
Description: Perfect place around Marathahalli to have Andhra style cuisines. Ambiance is pretty good and makes it easy to go with your family. Price ranges are also comparatively affordable. I suggest Meghana if you want to have a heartful dinner with your family.

Author: Ravi Shankar
Description: Every friday I go out for dinner to this place with my collegues and some times I get disappointed when the food is not served hot. I even ask the waiter whether they have been serving the biriyani that was prepared in the afternoon for which he denies otherwise biriyani is very good!!!

Author: Navya Dewangan
Description: I ordered meghanas full meals.and it was not good at all.very costly for the price they r charging.no much variety of curries in meals.won't recomm.end meghanas meals

Author: Rishan Chakravarty
Description:
Just called up Meghana Foods to order, I ordered two biriyanis and the sum was around 360, they said they will deliver on or above 500 so I was just having a chat with my friend to order something else. I requested the "Gentleman" to wait for a min (even though I called up wasting my balance) , he just disconnected the call! That was RUDE! You should not fly higher if you're already in the sky!

PS: This is completely not related to the standard of food they deal in!

Author: Sandeep
Description:
the quality has deteriorated here like anything.
am a regular here for lunch and biryani is my main course
the quantity and quality have both reduced
they have an ambur biryani right opposite next to More store where I go now instead of going here.

Author: Shyam Seshadri
Description:
Very homely feel to the place (in other words, not upscale, fine dine) when you enter. Banana leafs FTW and the food that tastes superb!
Tried the paneer biryani, as I am a vegetarian, and have to say one of the few veg biryanis that was excellent. Truly demolished the entire thing by myself, and its a lot of food.

Highly recommend!

Author: Jyotsna Biswal
Description:
Good variety of biryanis . Ambience is not so good but good service. Price is very much affordable but taste is not so yum .
Most of the biryanis they serve, with chicken toppings so we don't get the aroma of hyderabadi biryani.

Author: Priyaaaa Nanda
Description: I love the biryani here even the veg tastes yum!! My favourite here is the egg biryani and the prawn chilli fries !! Ambience is really good... Surprisingly the take aways are good too ... A perfect place for a decent dining with some best Andhra cuisine :D

Author: Tony Thomas
Description: Delicious Andhra biryani. That's Meghana. Definitely worth exploring at least once. Rates are affordable. The ambience was not so great as so many people were waiting for the table.

Author: Aakanksha Khanna
Description:
Located in Marathahalli, it would be easy to pass this restaurant by like any other Andhra restaurant. Thanks to a friend's suggestion, i stopped by and tried the food here. A good decision, as it does not disappoint!

We came here on a bustling Friday night and ordered chicken guntur kebab and two chicken boneless biryanis. The first thing i liked was the banana leaves - gave me the typical mess-type feel despite being in a cozy restaurant! Coming to the food, the kebabs were very liberally spiced and packed with flavor...yummy! The biryani was good too, esp with the salan and raita. The quantity was more than enough for someone like me who hardly eats rice.

Overall a very satisfying experience :)

Author: Shubhra Dixit
Description:
Five star because until I ate at Meghna in marathalli, I did not like biryani at all. But this place has made me fall in love with what is probably the best use for rice!
You can smell the goodness of their food from about 300 metres before you arrive their. And that aroma is the best form of advertisement for a restaurant.

Surprisingly, the paneer biryani is amazing. And no, veg biryani is not pulao, it is delicious and it IS a biryani that can hold its own.

My friend had the boneless chicken biryani which was even tastier than the paneer one by a notch or two. Another friend had the meals - spicy, tangy, flavourful, and the best andhra style meals I've had so far.

Special brownie points for the Raita (curd preparation) that accompanies the biryani. Just the right consistency, and refreshingly tasty.

Author: Shashank
Description:
Too spicy
I had biryani and i was actually crying cause of spice
Its andra food so may be few people like it but in general people cant take that much spice

Author: Kanheiya Agrawal
Description: Many people ,especially non vegetarian and south Indians will like this place very much..as a vegetarian and less spicy food lover for me this place was like hell. .all the dishes were so spicy that i have to drink 5-6 glass of water..so if you dnt like spicy food then dnt go this place. ..

Author: Ravi Bagaria
Description: There is one dish that i have loved here and will love it for ever . And i dont need the ambience , the service and the other stuff . lovely biriyanis . thats all

Author: Rakesh Patni
Description: Veg Biryani(especially paneer biryani) served here is awesome. My experience with biryanis had not been very good before going to  Meghana. Less bucks and looking for quality biryani , this is the place to be. However disappointed with the services. The marathahalli outlet was running short of napkins in the washroom. :P

Author: Prathibha Das
Description:
Worst hospitality ever!!!!! Mutton biryani is, for some reason, never there.. Food quality has drastically dropped over the years. Service is worst. Have had one of the worst experiences being here... Wouldnt ever suggest this branch to anybody.

Would have rated these bunch of losers "0", but had to choose "1" as its the least accepted rank on Zomato.

Author: Geetika Pandya
Description: The definition of a beasty meal. I ordered an egg biryani and a beverage, plus 3 of us thought we'd be able to share a starter along with our individual biryani orders. Well, how wrong were we! The biryani tasted good as did the starter. My friends' mutton biryani was brilliant, according to them. So overall, it was a good meal, with a lot of leftovers. Basically, if you aren't too hungry, 3 people can easily be full in a bowl of biryani and a side dish. Also, they are very prompt in their service. Kudos to them, there as well!

Author: Akash Rath
Description:
This is a pure andhra style restaurant. I like the Hyderabad biryani here ( both chicken and mutton). One speciality about this restaurant is they serve food on banana leaf.
Service- super fast
Food- andhra style delicious
Ambience- casual hangout friendly
Price- Totally pocket friendly ( ₹450/- for 2)

Author: Jagadeesh Rampam
Description:
Slowly my interest towards Meghana foods is reducing bcoz of the quality of taste of food and service!

I have taken my bro's family and a friend to this Marathahalli outlet on a Sunday evening. I could see lotsa crowd since Meghana foods is quite famous, when ordered for 3 Meghana's spl and veg biryani with extra dum, first they misplaced dum and have to remind them! Next food is salty not tasting good!!!!!!

Soft drinks ordered they misplaced and have to go with what they've offered, otherwise it would take a lot of time to change the order as you don't see order guys anywhere near to you!!!

Disappointed my ppl are as I have spoke high ad recommended strong about this place!

Ambience : 2/5
Food : 2/5
Service : 2/5

Author: Tridisha Goswami
Description:
This place has never disappointed me. And I would say that this is the BEST BIRYANI in Bangalore. I love the Chicken Boneless Biryani. Its well spiced with good quality rice and something that leaves you craving me more. 
The mutton pepper fry is another favourite. Soft, succulent pieces of mutton well cooked and a perfect accompaniment to rice. I also love the Guntur Chicken. Very spicy but i yet love to eat it while i shed tears. 
My husband loves their veg biryani and he claims its the best he has tasted.

Nothing much in terms of ambience but i have no complains for its service. A place i would go again and again just to feel full and happy! :)

Author: Sumit
Description:
Nice joint.. Fully packed.. The food is nice.. Service is good.. They serve you on banana leafs!
The food of this outlet is not the same as indiranagar outlet. Indiranagar is better. We ordered mutton biryani but it was a bit disappointing. The mutton was wrapped in excess of spices.

Author: Sanjay Bhagat
Description: I have been here a couple of times for lunch ... Had their biryani ... Good experience not very good ... Fine place to go once or twice ...

Author: Tanuj Sharma
Description:
It's a bit crowded place better go around 12:30 pm to get place. Mostly you will find office crowd here. Taste wise as usual biryani is too good... As I am a veggie so I have ordered paneer biryani. Which was mind blowing biryani as compared to other biryanis available in Bglor. It's a must try biryani centre. Biryani rice served with paneer gravy and lots of paneer on it yummm... It's a treat for paneer lovers.

Non veg biryani was ordered by my colleague which also got similar kind of review they were happy to have it.

Veg lovers: Must try paneer biryani once and I bet you will have it again.

Ambiance wise not so happening. A very simple one.

Author: Saurabh Araiyer
Description: A good restaurant. If you are allergic to spices better ask waiter before or you will end up drinking tonnes of beverages than eating. Innovative Biriyanis served. Like the place. Easily accessible by public transit and well managed.

Author: Mahesh Lohithakshan
Description: Awesome ambience, great service and some fine biriyani. Try their Meghana special biriyani, that's the best of the lot. Slightly overpriced though.

Author: Jyoti Agarwal
Description:
I regularly come here as it is close to where I stay. The food is great here. We normally go for biryanis and Andhra meals. The quantity is sufficient for two people for the biryani.Also the starters and lassis are great. The service is very quick and the place is very pocket friendly :P The only con I find is the ambience is pretty ordinary, most of the days its very crowded.
But overall a good place if you are really hungry and you dont want to spend much :)

Author: Bobby
Description: I Ordered Biryani. They haven't provided the lemons and onions. WTF? They are charging lot more money and not providing these stuff .. Fat idiots

Author: Kailas Senan
Description: Very nice food we have today from Meghana. We tried both veg and non-veg items, starters were so good and spicy. Chicken and Vegetable Biriyani, Chicken Fried rice were so tasty.

Author: Shraddha Agarwal
Description: My all time best place is meghana.. same as other place but not sure why is there a difference in price in this place when we compare it to kormanagala's MF. Overall nice place and yes the best part -  meghana special chicken biryani

Author: Raghu Saboo
Description:
We were on the way to Nagarjuna Indira Nagar, our fav. Andhra style restaurant. Made a quick u-turn near the old airport on seeing jam which I believe was due Shiva temple on occasion of Shivaratri.
Quickly checked Zomato for other Andhra options & found Meghna Food having descent reviews.

This was a 3.5+ had they not served me cold biryani. We had a veg biryani & a veg meal. Both were above average.

Author: Kushagr Gupta
Description: Awesome finger lickin' food but horrible home delivery experience. It is a good place to personally go and eat but trying to get home delivery is a nightmare. The attendants don't know how to talk and seem completely uninterested to even take the order. Bad experience.

Author: Shri
Description: Delivery is worst ! Number is always busy! I think so owner is talking to girl friend ! I hate this behavior! This is not fair to do people who are very hungry !

Author: Nelson N
Description: Had a nic time with Ma frnds after 3 years .. One of Ma Frnd requested to go to this place but Need to walk a little from brigade .. But food was ok v enjoyed it good keep it up ..

Author: Divya K
Description:
Good place for Andhra food. Biryanis are good here.
But this place is always crowded and if you go there in peak time during weekend and if you are two people you would end up sharing the table with other people. We went there twice and we had the same experience both the times. It could be better if they can have a few small tables which can accommodate 2 ppl.
We tried their meals. It was pretty normal nothing great. Taste is good but items were less. It would be better if they can add some more items to their meals menu. I would suggest a fry curry, roti or puri and may be a one more gravy. This can be a special meals with a little higher price. This is just my suggestion. This is what I expected when I ordered meals.

Author: UT_91
Description: Meghana doesn't need a review honestly the name is enough but still for those who haven't visited this place please go and dine in here either in koramangala or marathahalli . Chicken boneless biryani and prawns biryani is a must try . value for money is too good. Service is superb ...spicy and awesome food in short !

Author: Saideep K
Description:
One of the Best Biryani's that I had after a long time. 
Been there with my friends suggestion for dinner with my friend. We ordered for Meghana Special Biryani and some Guntur chicken as a starter. As by the name, Starter was so spicy, We managed to finish it with some soft drink, and then we got Biryani which we are waiting for. It was so tasty, Perfect spicy and all the masala's. I loved it and seriously recommend for al biryani lovers out there.And our Dinner ended with a Lassi which is also very decent. 
Service, Ambience and all are good, I mean no one actually care about these things, when they had this biryani in-front of them :D

Author: Srishti Gupta
Description: I was here on recommendation by a friend for good chicken biryanis. A regular place with decent food and service. Someplace to drop by if you happen to be around this area but not worth planning a trip to.

Author: Vinay
Description:
best place to have biriyani.

Boneless chicken biriyani is my favourite.

but dont order  lime soda/water they server it with normal temperature

Author: Barsha Biswas
Description: This place is very close to my home and office. So whenever I crave for some quick heavy, spicy food I can just drop by or order home delivery. It is very cost effective and the biriyani portions are huge. The service is very quick, you hardly have to wait for 5 minutes for your order to arrive. Not much of an ambience; it is the sort of place where you are expected to come, eat and leave as the place is always crowded during meal times. I would highly recommend the Meghana special biriyani.

Author: Ajit
Description: We've been meghna koramangala fans for as long as we can remember. When it opened close to marathahalli it was such a relief as we didn't have to trek all the way to koramangala. But we were in for a rude shock. Went on a Tuesday afternoon for lunch. Wanted to try out their andhra meals for a change. We entered at 3pm and asked the manager on the floor whether meals were available and he said yes. We were shown a table and then the surprises began. I was served cold, like fridge cold rice. The sambhar was cold and purely water, nothing else. No vegetable was served. When we raised a concern about this a very very rude and disgusting manager came and said that the time for meals is over and that's why we were being served this crap in the name of food. We only said that you should be informed us when we asked and we would be ordered for something else. They didn't bother to apologise and we demanded one of the meals to not be charged and he accepted. On providing the bill, the meal was charged for and the cocky manager says," I gave you hot sambhar so I will charge the meal. If you don't want to eat hot food, that's your problem!". He then had the audacity to tell us to leave if we don't want to eat food. Shocking and disrespectful!

Author: Janarthanam S
Description:
On saturday night, me and my friends wamted to try some restaurant in marathahalli, so we searched for the place and atlastwe have decided to go meghana Foods, Since it is a andhara style  restaurat
              We have reached the restaurant around 9:30 Pm. it was crowded, so the waiter asked me to go second floor of the restaurant. we went upwards and got the seat immediately. Ambience is good and lighting effect feels good. I am happy to see that banana leaf was there instead of plate. Since it is good to have food in banana leaf.
             we have ordered the two starters Tandoori chicken and meghana chicken 555 to start of, unfortunately this restaurant doesn't provide the soups. The waiter brought us the starters, both items are good especially meghana chicken 555 was better in those two items.
           Then we have ordered prawns biriyani and egg biriyani, Biriyani rice was good in both the biriyanis and it was spicy as we expected. Prawns seems good and doesnt given any smell as usually it does. Raita was really good. Still we felt bit hungry finally we have ordered chicken mughalai curry and four rotis one for each person. Chicken mughalai had more chicken pieces than gravy. Taste was good.
       Overall it was good place for those who want to have spicy andhara style foods, if you looking for these kinds of food u can try this restaurant for sure

Author: Madhava Chandra
Description:
Not sure if they are authentic Andhra or Hyderabadi. They seem to be a mix of everything.
I suspect there is a tad bit of Soda that is added to the food here or it was the semi- cooked Biryani rice that led to a bad gastronomic experience.
I never found the Biryani to be one of the best here. I prefer Nizam's Kitchen to the one I tasted here.
Many a times had it ordered to work but never enjoyed it as thoroughly as Nizam's.

Author: Food Freak
Description:
The food is good just like their older branches. Had heard a lot about this place but no one ever mentioned the pathetic customer service provided by them. Every time we call for placing order for home delivery, they create one or the other problems. First of all they dont respond to calls until we call them 2 or 3 times . Even after picking up they treat us as if we are indebted to them for something. 
Please learn to treat customers properly. I am never going to order from Meghana Biryani especially the Marthahalli branch.

Author: Akhil Ramesh
Description: Extremely bad experience. Reached here because we didn't get a place in Absolute Barbeque. Food was oily. Ordered Kadai Veg curry and Butter roti. Both were extremely oily. Very unhealthy food. Ordered Andhra Meals. Was very normal. Ambience and service was plain and boring

Author: Sushmita Agarwal
Description: I would rate this place 2.5 because the ambiance is too bad. Infact this is just an old two floored building. Looks very unhygienic. However, the Biriyani really surprised me. Once you taste it, you won't care about the ambiance or rather lack-of-ambiance at all. Great food.

Author: Nidhi Pandey
Description: Not a place for ambience but definitely a place for value for money good meal. First time had been there to try meghana special biryani while the second time just returned as the taste lingered. A little disappointed with the service in the 2nd visit. You don't recommend a prep and later say sorry we don't have it. Chilly fish is the worst thing i have had till date, will still give lots of marks to special biryani. Realised one more thing, keeping the biryani one day and eating it another made it tastier ????

Author: A P Patro
Description: I only had Chicken Biryani here. Sumptuous Biryani was served on a banana leaf. Biryani seemed to be cooked in the famous Andhra style. Good place to have weekend appetisers

Author: Rajasri Nanduri
Description: This restaurant does not have the Hyderabadi biryani one would expect on hearing Andhra biryani but the authentic taste of Andhra biryani that's available in the rest of Andhra Pradesh. Service and ambience were okay. Exclude all these non-food-related-okay things and you have the best biryani providing restaurant in bangalore. Try the boneless chicken biryani for no surprise but delicious biryani.

Author: Krishna
Description:
Been there after hearing a lot.
Place is conjusted n almost pilla like a normal mess... Food is not good.. Biryani was coloured and tasteless.. Not sure why people hype a lot

Author: Pradeep Kumar
Description:
I had been to meghana in Koramangala and Maratahalli.
After having lunch in Maratahalli, i feel that meghana maratahalli
is not a original branch of Marathalli.

Once i ordered Tandoori chicken it was cooked well but became little black and getting too much roast smell

Author: Pranoy Mitra
Description:
For the price this place is a real steal. They have become my staple food source on weekends and on national holidays.

I personally don't prefer to have food over there and use them as a pick up joint.
The quality has been good, service is sometimes bad due to over crowded place.

Chicken biriyani , chicken 65 and fish fry are my pick of the lot.
Chicken kadai is not that good though!!!!

Author: Prathikh
Description:
Won't recommend anybody to visit this place.
Short vesion: staff doesn't care about customers, nor do they admit to their mitakes, staff is not up-to-date about the prices of the items that are not listed on the menu (they'll tell you x Rs and charge x+y Rs). Also, I was served almost uncooked chicken and threw up twice due to that.
If you're interested in details, please read further.
I went there with two friends on Sun 4th jan 2015. We ordered one meghana special biryani, one paneer biryani and the attendant suggested to take that day's special desi murga biryani. We asked the price of the desi murga biryani since it was not on the menu, he told 200/-. We said ok. Finally when the biryani's came, the paneer biryani was kind of ok, so was meghana special biryani but desi murga biryani had pieces of chicken which had not been cooked properly. I told them, and their response was pathetic. The attendant said "this biryani is prepared like that only, the chicken is kept a little crisp". I told him that I can clearly feel it's not been cooked properly. But they were not at all ready to admit their fault.
That's not all, finally when the bill came, we had been charged 220/- for the same pathetic biryani. I called the attendant again and enquired about the difference in the price, to which he said after confirming with the billing staff that the price had increased two days back. Seriously, price changes in your restaurant and you don't know about it?
Now comes the worst part. We left after paying the bill, on the way back home, i threw up twice because i couldn't digest the uncooked chicken.

Author: Priyabrat Das
Description:
Well, I was there to taste the much talked about Meghana special Biryani and was not disappointed. It's differently prepared but definitely one of the best biryanis one can get. Ambiance can definitely get much better but I didn't quite give it much attention as I was busy with my biryani.

One can come here to taste a differently prepared yet tasty biryani.

Author: Kotni Srikant
Description: The first time I came to Bangalore my friend ordered meghna special biriyani  for me and he ensured me that this would be best biriyani he would be offering me and seriously its the best one I have ever had.

Author: Pavitran  Padathaki
Description:
Will not recommend this place to anyone to spend a lazy weekend with decent ambience

Heard a lot about Meghana so I thought of giving it a shot.

Food was not that much great except the boneless biryani , rest of the dishes what we ordered was spicy and service which lacks passion and experience.

Author: Anil Kumar Karamsetty
Description: I have been to his restaurant many times and food was really good all the time. I like egg fried rice very much in this restaurant. We ordered different starters and all very tasty. Anyone looking for a spicy and tasty food this is a good option.

Author: Nipuna Ranjan
Description: Very good quality of biriyani for non-vegetarian (both fish and chicken ) . Fast home delivery within 3 kms  . But most of the time crowded .

Author: Harish Chandra Singh
Description: We have tried meghna food several times specially biryaani. I think biryaani is really good. Apart from that I have also tried veg meals and some nonveg items like chicken chettinad and mutton masala and that was also taste good though all the items was more spicy but I don't mind as I like spicy food. Only one thing I feel little odd is thy put lot of pieces and curry on top of biryaani which somewhat suppress the real taste of biryaani itself.

Author: Sanket Panda
Description:
Non-veg delight and nightmarish spicy
that sums up my experience
First of all The Biryani at the Hyderabad Biryani chain of restaurants is way lot better than what Meghna serves
The chicken roast was heavenly and the best dish i ordered
Vegetarian options are terrible ..
They should understand that making everything spicy doesnot make food tastier
If u love super spicy food..look no further..
Otherwise avoid

Author: Babu
Description: Had been to this place for dinner with family and friends, loved the ambience and hospitality, initially took some time for the order to arrive, but the food was awesome, especially the mutton biriyani was yum, chicken tandoori was very soft and cooked very well, kadai paneer gravy was mouth watering..overall was a good experience..

Author: Reshma
Description: Lemon chicken is delish-totally must try! Meghana special biryani is awesome too..it's different - boneless chicken pieces as a side to the biryani rice..very tasty!

Author: Abhisek Mallick
Description:
Went to this place last Saturday with my fiance as she was interested in Andhra Food.
We reached the place approx 9.45pm. We ordered Andhra Style Chili Chicken, Mutton Biriyani and Chicken Biriyani.
About the Andhra Chili Chicken - it is spicy, I mean serious spicy. We expected it would be spicy and was happy to see our expectation full-filled.
The biriyani was good in quantity and the chicken/mutton pieces were properly tendered.
However the reason why I rated them low was for their service.
I haven't finished my food and my fiance was during middle of her meal when they brought wash bowls!!!! If that was not enough the moment I finished my meal they brought the bill and kept on standing their till I paid. And it wasn't just me it happened to 2-3 tables next to me. Looked like they wanted to shoo away their customers fast!! And the time was around 10.20 pm.
This isn't a right way to treat customers, give them proper time to finish their food and relax they wont runaway without paying.

So here is my final rating
Food Quality - 3.8/5
Food Quantity - 4/5
Ambiance - 3/5
Service - 2/5.

Author: Jitendra Kumar Dash (JkD)
Description: The worst biriyani ever. The veg foods are nice though but its really a "waste of money and time". The place is always crowded I don't know why may be they marketed well with their quality in past.

Author: Sabya Shaikh
Description: Awesome biryani :-* . Spicy yet tasty! ... Food is served on banana leaves.... And not to forget the lemon chicken... Twas yummy! Quantity is good enough!

Author: Rohit Sinha
Description: Was here for second time .this time for team lunch. As lot of orders were there i got to taste many chicken items .almost every starter we had was delicious . after that we had biryani which was also good.  It was very crowded but taste of food compensates for any downside. Overall a good place to eat

Author: Manuraj Chouhan
Description: Good place for biryani and chicken lovers . I had chicken boneless biryani their. It was good and they serve biryani in a different style. My friend had chicken fried rice it was also pretty awesome.

Author: Vineet Chauhan
Description: Had boneless chicken biryanis and it was pretty well made with great taste. Quantity was decent and chicken was too good. Would  suggest everybody to go Meghna Foods atleast once and eat a lot of stuff especially biryanis...

Author: Vinesh Koka
Description:
I have been a fan of their boneless chicken biriyani, right from their initial days of being a crammed little outlet in Koramangala with no branches. This outlet has lots of seating space and was still packed on a Friday afternoon, numbers swelling by the minute. The food arrived quite fast, tables cleared up soon enough, and everyone seemed happy to wait for their turn to sink their teeth into the Meghana specials!

The CHICKEN PATHANI KABAB (₹390), GUNTUR CHICKEN (₹185), and LEMON CHICKEN (₹185) proved to be very effective starters. The Lemon Chicken, though, looked brown in colour and wasn't tangy as expected. It tasted good, but it well may have been another dish, we didn't ask. As always, loved the MEGHANA SPECIAL CHICKEN BIRIYANI (₹181), but also tasted the MUTTON (₹190) and PRAWN (₹199) variants. I think I'll try the prawn version next time, for variety; tasted very good. I'm not too sure what the difference between the Boneless Chicken and Meghana Special Chicken is, but it is not much - both overall spicy and tasty. The chicken pieces are placed on top of the biriyani rice. The quantity is a lot, though. Three people can eat two biriyanis.

As I broke into sweat (this usually happens when I eat something too spicy, especially at an Andhra-style restaurant), I kept eating and panting while continuing to eat some more. At one point, I had to throw in a bit of sugar into my mouth to neutralize things; for there was more wonderful biriyani left to be eaten!

TIP: While ordering, specify 'less spicy' if you like it that way; it seems they can control the spice level (the waiter told me this when I asked him for the sugar. I don't remember it being that way in Koramangala)

Author: Shankar Manickam
Description:
Degrading quality day by day

I visit Meghana once in a week as I work close by.

But the quality of the food is degrading day by day and I can sense that in last 3 visits.

We were 4, ordered 1 starter, 3 Meg spcl biriyani and 2 sweet lassi.

after 10 minutes, we got 2 lassis--SALT.

we informed to the manager and he brought two sweet lassis.

then we got the biriyanis(no starters again).

Biriyani rice was hard and it was not that easy to chew, seems like previous day rice kept in the fridge.

The chicken piceces in it was clearly noticeable that it was frozen. It was not smooth and hard to eat too.

Finally we got our finisher or starter thru which we finished our lunch. starter was late because it was made with fresh chicken!!!

Let me not go to Meghana again!

Author: Vjr Chowdhary
Description: The food is brilliant and very filling. For vegetarians, try Paneer Biryani for once. Its out of the world. The Andhra Thali is also amazing. From what I have heard, chicken biryani is very flavor-full. I tried peeping in their kitchen and it was Spic-and-Span clean.

Author: Teja Priyanka Yadav
Description:
Ever since I moved to Bangalore, I have had an on-again-off-again affair with Meghana. While I was a regular at Meghana foods, Koramangala; it was a drive of 10 kms from where I live. But well, their biryani always made it worth for me.

I was more than elated when Meghana opened their outlet in Marathahalli but my first experience and every experience ever since has been kind of a let down. While I never had to wait for a table here, I feel that the quality of food served here isn't quite the same as the one in Koramangala.

Their home delivery option tests your patience and takes 2 hours to deliver on a weekend and 1 hour on weekday.

But complain as I might, I still religiously go here and get my share of Boneless chicken biryani with their chilli chicken (dry, chinese style) and butter milk. I also really like their Andhra thali, which comes complete with vulava chaaru.

Their service at the restaurant is speedy quick. Parking is always hard to find on that particular stretch of service road, you might get parking space near Ford showroom on the same side of the service road or you could park near 'More' on the opposite side and just cross the ORR.

Value for money - 4/5
Will I go here again? - For the love of biryani, yes!

Author: Ajay Damodaran
Description:
I had gone along with a group of 12 people to Meghana Food's Marathahalli (Opposite Absolute Barbecue). The experience was pathetic to say the least.

We had ordered 2 plates of chicken 65 as starters and that's when the waiter recommended another starter by the name of Chicken Mangolia. He said that it's their special dish for the day and It's very popular. We thought we'll give it a try. Finally when the starter was served the so called "Chicken Mangolia" turned out to be a plate of old, stale and foul smelling chicken pieces. Most of the pieces were those that are normally not used for making curries or starters. 

The tandoori rotis were cold and hard. We were waiting for a couple of friends to arrive so we requested them to bring the main course a little later. They were hellbent on bringing it immediately as if they were desperately waiting to feed us and leave for a movie or something. The service was miserable. It was evident that the dishes were all old since they didn't take more than 3 minutes to bring our starters.

 I urge zomato to please blacklist this particular restaurant and request all fellow zomato users to avoid this restaurant unless you want to end up having a disastrous lunch/dinner.

Quality 1/5

Quantity 3.5/5

Cost 2.5/5
Service 1/5

OVERALL 2/5

Author: Akhil Gupta
Description:
*The Lord of Aroma and Flavor*

You enter the restaurant, take a flight of stairs and an aroma of spices hits you to awaken you. Then smartly dressed stewards lead you to your table. Almost every table has one guy who will permanently take care of your order, refilling water, etc.

Brilliant service with very courteous stewards. I'd like to give a special mention to all those who keep standing and take care of your every order and clear off the plates and get the finger bowls. All the stewards are very nice and they'll describe the dish and suggest you what to try.

The food is brilliant and very filling. For vegetarians, try Paneer Biryani for once. Its out of the world. The Andhra Thali is also amazing. 
From what I have heard, chicken biryani is very flavor-full.

I tried peeping in their kitchen and it was Spic-and-Span clean. 

To sum up, If you are in the city, go and checkout this place for yourself. They are serving one of the best Biryanis. And for the Happy endings, there is a paan-waala just downstairs.

Author: Abhisek Pradhan
Description: Was there for the first time and looking at others mostly ordering biriyani even I followed suit and ordered boneless chicken biriyani. Service was very quick(biriyani orders are usually fast). The boneless chicken biriyani looked different from the usual biriyanis. It had a separately fried boneless chicken over the biriyani rice and it tasted reallyyyy yummmmmmmy(this shows how much I loved it). The fried chicken tasted so good I requested for some more gravy of it and the waiter was very kind to oblige. Would suggest everyone to try boneless biriyani atleast once, I bet you are gonna love it !!

Author: Abhiroop Dutta
Description:
Yet another savior for our late night deliveries to office. One plate of Meghana Biriyani is more than enough as they say.
Somehow I felt that the quantity has reduced. However, I do believe they cannot match the quantity of local Hyderabad biriyani places.
The quality was not as good as their koramangala or jayanagar branches.
Quantity 3.5/5
Quality 3.5/5
Cost 3.5/5
OVERALL 3.5/5

Author: Ashmeer S
Description:
This is the second outlet of Meghna Food I have personally visited.

We stuck to the famed boneless chicken biryani. Yes the servings are enough. Two plates served for three and there was enough for each one.

Service is tad slow and guess communication of staff an issue. However this outlet takes care of the inadequacy of space at Koramangala outlet.

Finding this could be a challenge. A better landmark is to check for Sony Centre.

Author: Kish
Description:
After hearing some good feedback from some of our friends
I picked up biryani here and like many restaurants in Marthahalli the biryani here is studded with full of colors and chicken pieces with no taste.Only good thing I found was the quantity of the food which is use less if its not tasty.

Bottom line :

Much hyped biryani joint in Marathahalli.

Author: Sankhdeep Mitra
Description:
It's been a while since I remember leaving from a restaurant feeling truly satisfied on all counts--quality of food, value for money, ambience, service, and hygiene. Fortunately, Friday lunch with the team at Meghana Foods, Marathahalli delivered exactly that!

Our team of nine started proceedings with chicken pathani kabab, lemon chicken, and guntur chicken. Each item offered a distinct state and the pathani chicken kabab was the winner for me.

Billed as an Andhra biriyani place, we sampled a sizeable range of them--boneless chicken, Meghana's special, mutton, and prawn.

Each biriyani had a generous helping of meat, yes, even the prawn one! The meat and the masala is mostly on top and the fragrant rice is underneath. If you're expecting this Andhra biriyani to be the same as the one you get in a Nandhini or Nagarjuna, you're in for a surprise. Yes, it's spicy and served with generous bowls of raita and gravy, but that's where the similarity ends. It's tasty and unique--that's all that matters!

Helpings of sugar, sprite, and coke inevitably followed. But nobody was complaining.

The staff did a phenomenal job at service despite the booming crowd. During lunch hours on Friday, the people waiting for a table will swell exponentially. So make sure you arrive no later than 12.30 PM if you hope to get a table without jostling for space.

In summary, this place is highly recommended and I'll certainly be going back for more.

Author: Amogh Ravikumar
Description:
Went there in the afternoon Rush Hour.....There was a pretty big queue and we thought we were done for. But to our surprise the queue dissipated quickly and we were shown to our table in just 10 minutes. And the whole process of order receiving to serving is very efficient and quick. I am very impressed. 
Service: 4.5
Food was delicious, especially meghanas special chicken biriyan and its very affordable considering its taste.
Food : 3.5
And dont think much about ambience if you go on weekday afternoons cuz its usually a busy area and obviously it gets crowded.
Ambience: 3.5
All in all I would say Go for it!!

Author: Parvez Ahmad
Description:
Biryani was just yummy.. Happy with quality and Quantity.
the first thing i like about the place is that you can choose to have your food in a banana leaf (Which is one very strong reason why i go there) / you can ask for a plate as well if you are not comfortable eating in a banana leaf.

Author: Laxman
Description: The Veg Biryani is awesome here. The quantity was good enough so three Biryani fed us four full grown men.  The place is quite clean and best part is its not that expensive, infact its kinda cheap :) Thanks Meghana folks.

Author: Varun Singh
Description:
Good Place to Fill Youself Up !!!

We've been to 3 Meghna Biryanis and I like their consistency in taste and quantity.
They've got a pretty good idea - Make the rice and top it with different toppings cooked separately, that leaves the rice quite dry and fluffy!!

Very Good Taste and if you are in a group you are in luck everyone can order different flavours and share the toppings, the rice is same for all anyways.

Great Taste and really huge quantities !!!

Author: Saravanan
Description:
Timing:
Never go on a weekend! You got to wait behind those who are eating, served quick and literally forced to eat quick & kicked out such that others can occupy your seat! And the same treatment continues for the next one too! 

Service:
They are running the restaurant more like Andhra Mess, prompt service is good! But need to give time to eat! The guy served me the whole Biriyani bowl, one shot. Starters comes along with Main course - sometimes even later.

Taste: 
When I was in BTM, I used to visit the Koramangala Branch almost every week. The Biriyani used to be so yummy. With more branches now, the taste has taken a hit. At Maratahalli branch, I didn't even feel that I am having Hyderabadi Biriyani - very dry, half-cooked rice. I ordered Lemon Chicken (my fav in Koramangala Branch), but they served something else. When asked, he said it is the right one!

Author: Meetu Gupta
Description:
We heard a lot about Meghna Foods' Biryani and this weekend we were willing to have Biryani, so thought to try Biryani in Meghna Foods. We gave the order of

1 Chicken Kolhapuri
1 Nati Biryani
1 Butter Naan

The food was served on the banana leaves, as per the south Indian tradition.

Chicken Kolhapuri was very tasty specially the spices used to cook gravy was awesome. I loved the Chicken Kolhapuri there. Butter Naan was also fine. 

Nati Biryani was amongst the specials to the day. It was suggested to us the waiter there. So we ordered on his recommendations. But we didn't find it great. It was an average.

Service was also little slow. The distance between two dinning tables were also quite close together. Mouth freshener and bill came to our table before we could have completed our dinner.

So, I ll give 3.5 stars to Meghna Foods Maratahalli on the scale of 5.

Author: Ravi Teja
Description:
Very nice food...
Yummy... Never disappoints me...
I love the biryani... And Andhra style chilli chicken... The north Indian curries are also good...

Author: MELWIN
Description:
have tried the Meghna Special Chicken Biriyani a lots of time.. has enough quantity to fill your stomach.. spicy but felt it is a bit oily..
enormous quantity of rice in home delivered biriyani, took 2 ppl to finish one biriyani

Author: Bharati Chauhan
Description: Had a biryani a couple of times in koramangala outlet. Therefore had a high expectations. Marathahalli outlet is a big let down..ordered veg and non veg biryani for take away.. to my surprise both came as non veg. I wonder how come the kitchen staff can take this so leniently.

Author: Aparajita Rao Das
Description:
We strolled in around 2pm on a sunday afternoon and the place was packed choc-a-block. The wait time was about 15 mins. The service is really fast .
Now coming to the food , we ordered the meghana special biriyani and chicken rost which was the dish of the day.
The biriyani lived upto its name . But the chicken rost was extremely hot for our palate.
Overall this place is good to grab an awesome biriyani without burning a whole in your pocket but not a place to have a fancy date.
Food - 4/5 (1 point decreased for the extra spicy chicken rost)
Service - 4/5
VFM - 5/5Ambience - 3/5

Author: Deepak Nayak
Description:
The food is absolutely awesome. I have tried all types of biriyanis they serve and each time they are very tasty and better than the last time. Over the time I discovered they sell the chicken packets of boneless special biriyani separate for Rs 100. This was an absolute delight. Now a days I normally go and pick the chicken packets only if I want to eat light.

The only thing which is falling short of my expectation is the quantity. The quantity chicken in the packet has reduced significantly. It seems like Meghna has decided intentionally to reduce the quantity. No complaints on the quality, its still the best. Earlier two packs was enough for 3 people, but now we need to have 3. Please don't reduce the quantity, instead introduce the concept of family pack.

Author: Rajan Pattjoshi
Description: Went with my friend.Ordered meghana special biryani,lollypop biryani and golden chicken was very good in taste.loved the taste and cost was average.

Author: Gauraang
Description:
okay ! so i have been here multiple times and not once have i been disappointed... i have ordered random things from the menu and every dish has been a delight to my palate !!

the first thing i like about the place is that you can choose to have your food in a banana leaf (Which is one very strong reason why i go there) / you can ask for a plate as well if you are not comfortable eating in a banana leaf.

So here is the list:-
I have tried 4 different types of biryanis here and they were ALL amazing !! a special mention to their mutton biryani... its one of the best mutton biryanis i have had in the city !! their meghana special biryani is bone chicken coated with egg and then served with the biryani rice which is also a great combo for those of you who lik to experiment with taste a little bit.

the Rayalsima Chicken (or any andhra style chicken they have) was well made... had a special delectable flavor to it but let me warn you.. its a little spicy and you might wanna order a fresh lime soda sweet on the sides if you are ordering anything from the andhra style chickens ;-) their lemon chicken is really good and one good things bout their biryani is that its a burst of different spices and tastes which are in good balance with no particular taste over bearing the other !!!!

its really economical with really good helpings !!! go there and eat !!! :D

Author: Amrit Noronha
Description: the best andhra food. we were hungry.. the food turned out to my yummy. at the end it was cheap on our pockets too. good food, good day... for five people the food was ridiculously cheap. keep it that way :)

Author: Gautam
Description: We were very excited when heard bout that Meghana foods opened in marathalli. But with the quality of service specifically the parcel just bringing down the reputation . please try to improve the parcel service.

Author: Sipra Bihari
Description: The boneless biryani is worth raving about. The andhra style chilli chicken bowled me over. The only problem is this place is always crowded and you would have to wait for sometime before you get a table. The quantity served for biryani is humongous. Have been eating from meghana since 2010 (started from the near JNC outlet) and still recommend it to every biryani lover.

Author: Sravani Kumari
Description: This review is only for their paneer biryani. What we got was a bowl of biryani rice and a bowl of Paneer curry. we had to mix them both to get paneer biryani. wierd !!  Big chunks of paneer in some red gravy. Full of oil and red chilli powder. there is no masala. just the red chilly powder i suppose. though it tasted good, should be named as paneer rice.... Mushroom starter was good though!

Author: Saravanan
Description: One of the best biryani outlets in bangalore. The food is eccentric not to mention it's frugal pricing. The only disadvantage is the ambience hey you can't blame em when you have five hundred customers every hour.wonderful experience recommend to all.

Author: Basu Raj Agrawal
Description:
"Meghana's Biriyani - You haven't been there?"

Typically the first question any biriyani lover would ask you at Bangalore, but truly speaking, I wasn't very impressed by their quality of service, the food was good no doubt, but the service was just too lame! 
Had been on a friday afternoon and found it to be really rushy. They managed to give me a sit in one hour, for which they were not stretching on first come first serve but on number of people who have come with you! Something which none of us would love to see..
Talking about the food, the Biriyani rice was similar in all varieties, only the extra serving which was either a pack of vegetables or chicken changed. Make sure you tell them to make it less spicy if you wish to before itself! The food came in after 20 minutes, I must say it was hot and good to taste, and covered up for most of the mistakes they did previously.

I prefer you order a home delivery with a gap of about 1 hour prior instead of visiting this place. Biriyani is their major serving which people go here for - Just in case you want some menu based suggestion , If you find something else interesting, do let me know too :)  !!

Author: Sushovan
Description:
I had heard a lot about meghana's and i decided to try it out. So on a lazy Sunday afternoon me and my friend decided to go for take away. We order a Meghna special biriyani and a Butter chicken. The food was delivered to us in around 15 minutes.
The chicken Biriyani was not as good as expected. The biriyani rice and the chicken came in two different packets. The rice was good but the chicken didn't taste good.
The butter chicken also fell short of our expectation.
Overall the food was below average.
With Debashis Mohapatra

Author: Anubhav Chitrey
Description: Had veg biryani, and would say that it was the closest to what we call an authentic hyderbadi biryani, best of all the places I ever had biryani in bangalore. Served on banana leaves, place was crowded but service was really quick and staff was very cooperative.

Author: YUGANDAR PE
Description: One of the unfavorable food i experienced from Meghana foods in marathahalli, earlier meghana foods created its own idol in any kind of biriyani. But yesterday along with my few colleagues have visited meghanas food to get fine dine from them, but finally they disappointed us. The biryani which they served really its awful. The meghana 555 which they served really not fit to eat we have to through it in a junk. Ya finally i would like to tell that its so expensive too comparing with others. :(

Author: Soumi Chaudhuri
Description: This place is newly opened in Marathalli and we went to have dinner on a Sunday evening. Previously I have tasted their Biriyani from their Kormangala outlet and it was really spicy and tasty. The place was crowded but we quickly got our table. We ordered Paneer Tikka for starters which was quiet spicy but tasty as well. The paneer was tender and juicy. For main course we ordered one Boneless Special Chicken Biriyani. The taste was not as great as I had experience previously and the quantity was also less. I am not sure whether it was only for the weekend or the overall service has gone down. However worth a try for the Biriyani lovers..

Author: Arindam
Description:
I never dined in the marathalli one but ordered for home delivery. My first order was for chicken biriyani and chicken 555. I was a little disappointed with the chicken biriyani as the rice was not fresh and was smelling like very old rice. But the chicken 555 wasnice and unique in it taste. It will give you a taste similar to chicken alfam which is an afghani dish.
The second time Iordered tandoori chicken and chicken biriyani. This time the biriyani was perfect just the way i was expecting and matched its cousin Kormangala's taste.
Tandoori chicken was just ok nothing great.  From the limited visits to this place, i would rate the food as 4, ambience is 3, quality again a 3.5.

Dish of my recommendation - Chicken 555 and chicken biriyani (hyderabadi)

Author: Ruchir Bhagat
Description: Their biryani needs no mention. It's one the best biryani i have had. But the mutton was just ok, not what i was expecting from them. But something i really didn't like about this place were the waiters, rude and unfriendly. I have not experienced such waiters in any of the other Meghanas outlet. That apart, food was the same as any of their other branch.

Author: Anil Kumar
Description:
I appriciate Hospitality, quick service.
Bullet chicken is soft, you can try once but other Chicken items are bellow average...
Fish biriyani is good, spicy and tasty....

Author: Srikanta Raju
Description:
Went with my brother,ordered veg meals.
The cost for the veg meal is so high and no good food.
i have the worst veg meal in marathahalli,you can get a good veg meal in any andra style food restaurant under 50rs,but they are charging 135+tax for a single veg meal with 1 main course that also not good.
Dont try veg here

Author: Tonysv
Description: A pretty neat place to be if you want to have food urgently.  Neatly kept. Good food. Only drawback I would say is about their serving as it's not properly planned and un trained men.. I definitely liked their idea of keeping the chicken on top of the rice and serving.  It definitely avoids confusion for the servers to divide the portion between people and avoid embarrassment by digging to find out a portion of meat. Meghna special was not as good as the normal biriyanis which was a bit disappointing. ... They are known for Biriyanis. .....No parking space ..hence please search for one for having food peacefully. ...

Author: Laxman
Description:
Guys,
Been to Meghana with friends on a crowded Sunday,
As it is a andhra delicacy we thought of waiting for few minutes,
This is one of the best place to try andhra food, all their special items wer good, orders gongura chicken, lemon chicken and fish fry, all wet good,
I'm main course orderd Meghana special biryani, mutton and chicken dum biryani, mutton was sold out but remaining wer superb but not spicy though,
Overall a decent place to try andhra style food.

Author: Varsha
Description: One of the many opportunist who are there just to make quick money and have no idea of food. I was there at Meghana food Marathahalli. Ordered for Butter Chicken. It was full of chopped onions and tomatoes. Gravy was dry. It was horrible in taste. When I told them that this cannot be butter chicken they tried to convince me that this is how butter chicken is prepared. Biryani was also like any other shop, nothing great. Service was also extremely poor. Would not recommend  anyone to go there.

Author: Krishna Patil
Description:
Meghna Foods having their Special Biryani with Chinese type fried chicken with biryani rice, was not what I had expected. Though their Chicken 65 was quite tasty and I would have enjoyed it more only if it had more of meat on the pieces. Even the glasses had to be changed twice as they were oily and not clean and I just refused to have water in an semi cleaned glass. Even the cold drinks where not Cold as they had run out of stock from the freezer, so I wonder how it was even called a cold drink.

Probably they were having a bad day but it ruined my meal at least.

Author: Aju Nair
Description: I can easily tell, this is one of the best biriyani's in Bangalore..we have ordered Meghana Special Biriyani, Chicken Bonless biriyani and Meghana Chicken 555...Due to the good quantity we had Chicken Bonless biriyani and we asked the waiter to pack Meghana special biriyani..Service was good, I didnt find the Meghana chicken 555 in the menu and I read some good review regarding the same dish here in zomato..I was asking for Meghana Chicken 55 and the waiter was not understanding and he was asking me again and again..in the end I understood that it is 555 not 55..haaha..Again this is one of the good place where we can get authentic hyderabadi biriyani..

Author: Udian Patel
Description:
I heard a lot about this place from my friends. Went there yesterday. I was not disappointed. I had the standard andhra vegetarian lunch. The pappu, curry and chutney was excellent. I was a little disappointed by the sambhar and rasam.

My daughter had chicken curry and it was tasty. Overall a value for money place and I am sure I will visit them often.

Author: Anish
Description:
It happened quite accidentally that I dropped in here. Ordered for the Meghana Special Biriyani and Chicken Lollipop. I should say that all Biriyani lovers must definitely pay a visit to this place. The Biriyani tasted quite different with its special combination of spices. The mix of Rice and Chicken is a speciality of this place. And above all, the quantity served was worth the price. Chicken Lollipop was tender and crisp. Nothing more...taste it for yourself.

Overall, a fine experience and would love to get back to Meghana for the Biriyani.

Author: Vikas Suman
Description:
A true value for money meal can be enjoyed here.
Me and my friend had a full course delicious lunch under rs.500 which is quite rare in bangalore now a days.
The serving is done over banana leaves which really amazed me.
Food and service was great.
Overall a nice place for family meals.

Author: K PAVAN
Description: The best andhra style food in Bangalore, I have ever tasted. Bit costly, but value for your money.

Author: Lakshmi Narayanan
Description: I went there with my frnd for full andhra meal. I was a bit hesitant as I like pure veg places but this place was serving non veg also. Nevertheless, I found the food decent, in fact good and the price for the service and ambience as reasonable. The taste was authentic Andhra stuff. I have now decided to convince my family to visit it along with me next time.

Author: Suhaib Anayat
Description: Went there one weekday afternoon and it was full, waited for half an hour for a place. Coming to food, very authentic andhra food, brilliant biryani and chicken rayal seema is a must. If you are looking for a place of reasonable price with great food then it's the one.

Author: Benil Babu Mathew
Description: Went here on sat afternoon to have some good biriyani.. Well ! Sad to say but I was disappointed .. Had 'kattikondu' or some thing like that they serve specially on weekends .. It was 185 and had lots of chicken pieces .. But the pieces were hard and most of it were bones ! I hardly found any flesh ! The rice was ok.. But not upto the standard of a good Biriyani ! Nevertheless the service was good !

Author: Pooja Laksh
Description: Had been here with family for lunch on a busy saturday afternoon. Even at 2:30 there was lot of people waiting. After waiting for ten mins found a place to sit love the plaintain leaf experience. I ordered for full meals, paneer manchurian, nati kodi fry. Service was quick food was not hot except for the rice. The aloo fry was very bad since it was not hot almost hard like rubber. The dal and tamrind curry was very good. Food was not very spicy sweet was very bad milk paysaam. For 139/- per meal quality needs improvement.

Author: Sunil Shekar
Description: One of the worst service and worst ever food. I have had 3 times and all 3 times i had to vomit after my dinner. Too much of oil and added colour.

Author: Michael Bonaparte
Description: Went to Meghana Marathalli, all the way from Ramamurthy Nagar based on the hyped feed back given by many of my friends. DISAPPOINTED.... I specifically asked them for biriyani which was not of hyderabadi type, rice and gravy mixed and they gave me a similar biriyani.   Bought chicken and mutton biriyani.. Mutton was not soft... its typically same rice mixed with different types of meat... If you ask for boneless chicken its given with biriyani rice. There was no flavor in the rice...  I feeel its better to have it at Ambur biriyani at roadside than to have it here... Also its not worth the price at all.. I get a similar hyderabadi biriyani at curries and pickles for much lesser price...

Author: Franklin Muthusami
Description:
Hi... had been there for a lunch on a weekday. Ordered boneless biriyani. Was good. Flavour was consistent. Chicken well cooked. Serves 3 for order qty 2. Satisfies the need for ordering a dry item too. One good point is they dont charge extras as service charge & Vat.
Good ambience. Even the busy hour was catered well by the bearers.

Author: Somnath Jana
Description:
Meghana is now a very well known eatery specially for their biriyani's in bangalore. I had been to their kormanagala brunch many times. Now they are at marathalli ,near to my place so when ever we wish to have biriyani we call meghana. Well the place will be very crowded during lunch and dinner, so take away/home delivery is a better option. We order Hyderabadi/boneless/meghna-special chicken biriyani and i must say the taste is always good , long basmati rice, little spicy masala and tendered meat. You will definitely like their biriyani as all of my friends who ever tasted became a fan of meghna's biriyani. If you are a spicy lover ask for double masala. Apart from biriyani i have tried guntoor chicken and peper chicken were decent. Andhra style meal is ordinary. Price is reasonable and quantity is huge. Service is prompt so the home deliveries. 
Do i recommend : Definitely

Food:3.5/5
Service:3.5/5
Value for money: 4/5
Price:3.5/5

Author: Prinu George
Description:
Whenever I miss authentic Andhra meals I come down to this place. Not served in plates, only banana leaf which adds to the authenticity.
I had been to their MG road outlet and since then the food and their service stuck to my head which dragged me to this place.
Meals as usual had all Andhra favorites like palak pappu, Sambhar, rasam, fry, curd, ghee, pickles, dal powder etc.
We went for hot n sour soup and pepper chicken. The former was just okay but latter disappointed. The chicken wasn't crispy at all.
Overall, I would recommend this place for fun family lunch or dinner. Try their guntur varieties. This place can get crowded during weekends and so expect some waiting.

Author: Bhumi
Description: I am not a big fan of biriyani's. My friend took me to meghana as it is famous for biriyanis. She ordered Nattu kozhi biriyani and claimed it to be good. As far as veg is concerned, i would only give 3 rating. It was just to ok. But, chilly mushroom was awesome.

Author: Arjun
Description: One of the best biryanis on offer in bangalore. But apart from that the food is not that great. We ordered chilli chicken and paneer chicken and for the first time i felt that the veg dish was way better than the nonveg one.. Chilly chicken was very very dry and it was not spicy at all, even though we ordered for spicy. Then came the biryanis, veg and boneless chicken ones and they were lip smacking!!

Author: Sathya
Description: One of the best Hyderabadi Biryani in Bangalore. Ordered boneless chicken biryani & meghana spl chicken biryani (chicken coated in egg), special chicken ( methi chicken ) was awesome - meat fresh & juicy. Must try.

Author: Kanvita
Description: They just don't do any home delivery even at a distance of 1km even if the order amount is Rs400 . This is very disappointing and I would like to say that they should just not show any home delivery options at all when you cannot handle the cost or do not have the resources for it .

Author: Sachin Ghodke
Description:
Tried this place twice, good for non-vegetarians...! Found always crowded....
Ambience wise it was ok!
Less spicy, less oily..nice food! Try Meghana special biryani..!!

Author: Tushar Pant
Description:
I'll give 4 for biryani.
Though a hard core non vegetarian,I would take a break here and order veg biryani.its yum.
Having said that,non veg is delicious as well..just that veg takes a leap.

All 4 for biryani..rest a typical south indian place.

Recommended - Biryani all the way..

Author: Satish AK
Description:
I went to this place thinking that it is a traditional mess kind of environment but contrary to my thoughts I should say it was actually a mess turned into a handsome small-time restaurant.
Ambience doesn't pose a catchy kind of look but convincingly good enough for having food. Seating is better but not the best I shud say.
It is a typical Andhra food place and well known for the andhra meals option. I liked the table routine of having the preparedness of banana leaflet ready for getting served.
