We straight away ordered for our favorite andhra meals and it was served within 5 min and the feast starts with the best known order of pappu (yellow dhal), gun powder and tomato pickle. The we followed with regular dishes of sambhar, rasam and finally curd.
I should say it was an ultimate experience because I had good Andhra meals after a gap of nearly 4 months.
Service was prompt but needs improvement.
Cost factor is economical considering the meals option but the non-veg side is yet to be tasted. I will make sure that I will visit this place again for having the non-veg food as I had received some positive reviews.
Food - 4.5/5
Ambience - 2.5/5
Service - 3.5/5
Cost - 4/5

Author: Praveen R
Description: Ordered 2 bone less biryani but delivered two biryani wth out chicken , but the delivery boy collected full amount.. after we noticed that we have received only one chicken packet .. we called to the management one packet has missed in the delivery, they said that they are going to deliver it. But they have not delivered. Such a worst service i have ever seen.

Author: Sridhar
Description:
I was here on a weekday for lunch along with friends. Located on the opposite side of the multiplex there is no valet available and it is a bit of a challenge to get a parking on the service road.
Restaurant has 2 levels first and second floor. At 1.15pm first floor was already full so we got a table on the second floor. Ambiance wise this is better than koramangala and jayanagar outlets.
Menu is more or less the same across outlets.
we ordered pepper chicken dry which is semi gravy not exactly dry and goes well with some plain hot and steaming rice. For biryani we ordered the boneless variety and meghana special. We also ordered a chicken fried rice. The biryanis taste is very consistent and portions are reasonable. The spice levels are very high. The chicken fried rice was different and tasty. It had shreds of fried chicken which were crispy and tasted nice.
Overall a good experience and the bill was around 700.

Author: Nameesh (Fodyssey)
Description:
This chain of hotels has established itself as one of the best brands in Bangalore serving Briyanis. Gone are the days when people used to go only to ‘Hyderabadi Biryani’ to have biryanis, coz Meghana food has gone past in terms of the popularity. You can make out by looking at the people visiting the restaurant. I have had a mixed bad feeling when it comes to this restaurant. The Koramangala one being the best and most populate and super crowded, Jayanagar is so so I would say. Then comes the Marathahalli branch which is happening.

To my surprise, it is located bang opposite to the most popular eating spot in Bangalore, the Absolute Barbecues. Well well, I guess the fans of Biryani would goto this place matter on what items gets served at other places. Its just like my case when it was a weekend and I wanted to eat some rice as I had not had it since past 2-days. I got there and find that its situated on the 1st & 2nd floor of building. The irritating part is that there isn’t any lift there.

As I got, I see that it was filled with people waiting, those looking to enter inside, those coming out, basically it was full of life. And then I settled myself to sitting at a place and immediately the manager taking care of my side of seating gave the menu and then next 1-2 mts there was another asking for order. I guess they are also aware that people going there would have already decided what to eat.
Without hesitation I too ordered for the ‘Veg Biryani’ and ‘Paneer Biryani’. Along with that ‘Lime-sode’, which was not there as they had run out of lemon. That was a bit irritating coz its was just 9.45pm and still 1-1/2 hrs to closure was there. I think they should go and get it from outside inside of saying that they are out of lemons.
Anyways, ended up taking Sprite and Coke instead.

The biryanis were served in matter of 2-3 mts. Before that they giving the bowls containing ‘Gravy’ and ‘Raita’. Those items are unlimited and you can ask for any amount of it.  
Started by taking the Veg Biryani which tasted quite good. Was not quite happy with the gravy as is it did not seem spicy. I mean the biryani it should be less spicy but then the gravy should add more spice. But anyways, the taste was there. Just that I did not feel it reached to the level as the ‘Koramangala’ branch but yes better than the Jayanagar one.
After eating 1/2 of the veg biryani, took the Paneer Biryani which I think is the same just that you get the additional Paneer which makes it more heavier. I would say its fun to have it so that you can eat paneer. The paneer was decently cooked as was on the softer side.

Now, one things I like about the biryani here is that the quantity is quite perfect and you don’t feel that it is heavy on the stomach. One biryani for dinner is the ideal to have.  
I think I ended asking for gravy and raita 1-2 times, the person serving obliged by giving it to us. And also gave us some onions to eat along with biryani. Overall had a good time having dinner there.

My rating would be at 3.5 for this place. For all the reasons that they serve decent biryani. But yes I think the only challenge there with the crowd, they all seem to be in some kind of hurry. Or I am not sure if they think they are in some ‘Mess’ of ‘Hostel Cafeteria’, where in people just come and eat an go. Or may be its a bit too cramped up. Overall I they are pretty good. Just that I would want they to compare the tastes at different branches and keep it consistent.

Food for 2 would come around Rs.350 including 2 soft drinks.

My other ratings would be -
Food - 3.5/5
Service - 3.5/5
Ambience - 3.0/5
Drinks - 3.25/5

Author: Shilpi Singh
Description: The biryanis by meghna foods are ultimate.. It's pure andhra style food which will leave you craving for more each time you munch on a morsel. I would highly recommend you to get the food home delivered rather than you hopping out of your house and having it here.. As the ambience and place is a little let down.

Author: Manikanteswer Bagavati
Description:
Are you looking for a good flavored biryani place  in and around marathahalli , then you are at a good restaurant , although their are other options like Bhagini , shanmuka or suchitra , for people who prefer colored and flavored rice at a decent budget and good quantity meghana is a value for money and easily fills two people with decent appetite , don't forget to add a side dish like chicken tikka or their special fish curry or natty kozi curry , just as an add on as their curry with the biryani will be enough for one person only , although the rice as i have said earlier comes in decent quantity 

Must try : Meghana Bone less  biryani and fish fry curry or any day special curry as in that day

Author: Shubhi Agarwal
Description:
I have been to Meghana foods before for their delectable biryani. Though being a pure vegetarian, I used to visit just to taste the veg or paneer biryani there.
But visited the marathalli branch recently, and ordered Paneer biryani. But while eating, I was shocked to find a chicken piece in my veg biryani. This is absolutely reckless from such a reputed place, and me being a pure vegetarian, it's no less than a nightmare to find chicken on my plate.
Sorry, but never visiting this place again.

Author: Nithyanand Pai
Description:
This was a last minute plan that worked pretty swell
Caught in a 3:00 clock traffic jam our hunger was at an all time high and hopes at an all time low.When we made it to Meghana we were lucky that the biriyani which we came hunting for was still available..
Without wasting time we ordered two Chi boneless biriyanis and a plate of the famous andhra chilly chicken ...
What i really loved was the dishes were not overtly spicy ..The biriyani was well cooked and the pieces were tender and juicy
Now am not a big fan of spice but the andhra chilly chicken has always been an exception
This was hands down one of the best i ever sank my teeth into
A hygienic place coupled with polite waiters makes this a must visit for foodies
We left after a heavy meal to our next destination :- To crash onto our beds as we were full to the brim

Author: Suraj Sinha
Description:
Even after having a bad experience on my first visit, i gave an another try, but  this came out to be even worse.
1st visit: we ordered Chicken Lolypop as starter, and it came out to be tasteless,  it was not cooked properly, and was not able find anything good in it to eat. And that really made the rest food tasteless for me as even after complaining they didn't respond.

2nd Visit: this experience was a newfangled one. we ordered Chicken Biryani, after taking as long as 25 min,
  they came and said that the chicken in the biryani is over and will provide chicken cooked with different ingredient, and assured us that it will taste nice, so we said, lets give a try. And Guess what it was really worthless, wonder what took them so long to bring cold rice and weird(but not tasty by any stretch of imagination) chicken pieces.

So i think the admin people of the Restaurant should take care of the food Quality.
For me and my friend ,I am not going to give them an another try

Author: Sai Raghuveer
Description: Good food but not really liked the ambience. Even the service was okayish. Economical.

Author: Saket Srivastav
Description:
Meghanas Biryani is a non-veg lovers paradise. The place is certainly not what you call a great ambiance  but thats the last thing you should have in your mind when you go to any of the Meghana outlets. Must try is the chicken boneless biryani ( you can try meghana's special chicken biryani if you prefer a litlle less spice ). There are quite a few resturants which have come up now with this concept of serving small rounded chicken pieces in their biryani but Meghana remains the pioneer. As authentic as it gets when it comes to the real taste of hyderabadi biryani, add to that the sumptuous amount each portion contains along with the super quick service the staff provides there and Meghanas comes across as a must try for any Biryani lover! 

Ambience - 3/5 (the recent outlet at Marathalli is pretty spacious)
Food -5/5 (For the unique chicken biryani taste and value for money as well)
Service- 4.5/5( The joints are always crowded but still the service is super efficient)
Must Try - Chicken Bonless Biryani and Prawn Chilly

Author: Mritunjay Kumar
Description: I usually like Meghna Biryani and for the same reason, I called up Meghna foods at Marathalli to order the same. I was not aware of the delivery locations that's why randomly called up. The person who received the call did not talk properly. I understand that my location could not be in the list where Meghna Foods deliver, but there is a proper way of communicating the same to the customers. Being rude doesnt help and neither does it encourage the regular/new customers to visit the place. Even if the food is good, improper response could give a wrong impression and I want to suggest the same to Meghna Foods.

Author: Preethi Bhandarkar
Description: Super fast and neat service for a quickie lunch! Special mention: Hyderabadi biryani.

Author: Nagaraj R Nagu
Description:
Ambience place to have good food. They maintains the quality and taste of biriyani. Prefer to have Maharaja chicken as starter and  boneless chicken biriyani as main course. 

- Service is very good. 
- It would be if they increase home delivery distance.

Author: Sagarika Mishra
Description: Thus is the worst outlet of meghna foods. The chicken Biryani is worst when compared to other outlets...They don't have anything in sweet...no feedback form...and no managers around..as informed to me.

Author: Somya
Description:
As the caption says, this franchisee serves one of the best biryani in Bangalore. Had ordered from them a number of times and will do more in future. They have a number of franchisee all throughout Bangalore which are easily accessible.
They do home delivery but not sure if its from all the locations.

Author: Ayush Prakash
Description: Muton briyani and chicken rayalseema is osum here.

Author: Sanjib Mazumdar
Description: Went to Meghna Foods marathahalli couple of times. Food is served in Banana leaves which increases your appetite . The service is very fast, food quality is good, prices are ok and the ambiance is cool but the taste of all the items are same. You cant find much of a difference between the taste of chicken, prawn and fish Biryani. The taste of Hydrabadi chicken which is one of my favourite is quite different from other restaurants. The Guntoor chicken was good. Overall rating would be 3/5.

Author: Soumya Panda
Description: Awesome biriyani... Till i explore more i rate this as the best in town :)

Author: Chavi
Description: I went to Meghna Foods to have Andhra foods. I have gone to the Residency Road branch near Gateway hotel. I have ordered 'Prawns Biriyani' while my family members ordered chicken biriyani. i tasted both. chicken biriyani is OK and Prawns biriyani is just awesome. Quantity is enormous. The gravy also was yummy. I recommend this restaurant. Service is also excellent.

Author: Aditya Borkar
Description: I had heard so much about their Biryani through Zomato and wanted to try it...it was quite a distance for us to visit the place but still were craving for Biryani and took the trouble of going there all the way. Biryani was very bad and we did not like it all..This was our first and surely the last time at Meghna Food

Author: Deep
Description: Chicken Boneless biriyani and chilly fish are very good

Author: Deep
Description: Boneless Chicken Biriyani and Chilly Fish  are very good...

Author: Ashutosh
Description: Didn't like it much. As I am a vegetarian, so i ordered Two vegetables and  butter naans. Vegetables were not good in taste by the standard of north Indian food. I think they were of andhra style, that's why I may have not liked it. Ambience is not that good. Average restaurant for veg food.

Author: Saurav Srivastava
Description:
Went there on a Sunday evening after watching it being praised on the Rocky n Mayur show. Ordered a Boneless Chicken Biryani, a Meghana Special Biryani and one Sprite. The Boneless chicken biryani is definitely worth it. 

I have had biryani at several other places like bhagini, biryani zone, Nandhini but nowhere the Biryani is as good as at Meghana. Total bill for 2 came to Rs 401Food - 3.5Service - 2.5 the guy forgot the cold drinks from the order :(Ambiance - 3Value for Money - 3.5

Author: Priyanka Debnath
Description: I love biryani, it is something which I just can't stop. The biryani is quite good but the rice is bit dry. But still can be adjustable, because of the spice the use. So folks still it's thumbs up n do taste it.

Author: Mukesh Kumar Jena (CYGNUS)
Description: I think this is the best biryani hub that I have been to till now. The service is very good and they will try not to make you wait for your dishes. This place is little crowded during lunch hours so might have to wait for about 5-10 minutes sometime.

Author: Kiran Babu
Description: Went for lunch this weekend, which was not a great choice in terms of time. Gets really crowded during weekend lunch time. Food was great and service was pretty fast. The biriyani was not very oily and tasted good. The lunch also looked promising. Want to try that also some time soon but not on a weekend though....

Author: RahimRaja
Description: Restaurant is awesome and food is very hygiene and tasty, response and service is good. Cost is a bit high when compared to others restaurants surrounding but the quality is good enough. Nice place to sit, talk and eat.

Author: Movin Jain
Description:
I became fond of Biryani after visiting Meghanas Koramangala. Used to go there for lunch with colleagues at least once a week. Was really excited to hear that this new branch is coming up very near our office.

However, the Marathahalli branch is not as good as the original Meghanas in Koramangala. The ambience is slightly better, but the waiters and the service is worse. The waiting time is also higher.

But most of all, the biryani is not nearly as tasty as the one in Koramangala. So I would suggest, if you want the real Meghanas taste, go to Koramangala!

Author: Sumit Sinha
Description: I visited this place last week. It looked like another Meghana restaurant, but I felt the hygiene was below the standard. The spoon which was given to me was not properly washed. We ordered baby corn as starters which I felt like gobi, never mind, we also ordered sp. chicken biryani which I believe was not as good as the one they serve at kormangala center. I have been to two Meghna outlets apart from this one and this one was slightly disappointing. The other which I have gone apart from Marathalli and Kormangala is near Brigade road, which was also fine.

Author: Gopika AD
Description: Megna foods is THE ideal lunch destination for me and my collegues since it is close to my workplace. We come here frequently to enjoy the spicy "Boneless biriyani" :).  They also have mouth-watering starters. This is a must try place for people who work or stay closeby.

Author: AADITHYA SUJIT
Description: Always wanted some real spicy hyderabadi food ?? Then meghana foods is surely the place to be visited.    The biryani here is definitely one of the few best Hyderabadi Biryani you can get in Bangalore. Having a very average ambiance and service , the food here makes it up for all issues. The biryani here is top-class , especailly the boneless chicken biryani is simply amazing. apart from these there are other really nice and spicy starters they serve ( chicken 65 and chilly chicken being my fav). The place is definitely worth a visit for its rich tastes and flavours of their dishes. The one in koramangala gets very crowded at lunch timings and you can hardly find any place to sit. This branch has a bigger service hall and a better ambiance to dine in !

Author: Dineshkumar R
Description:
I don't believe in ranking of the hotels anymore because it doesn't deserve to stood on the top. I don't know whether it is about the branch or I got the poor experience.

And about the food, I have ordered chicken biriyani and methi chicken fry. Biriyani is nothing special and there is only ghee smell bit but not anything more. But the fry was good and o liked it.

About the servers over there can't even wait till I finish my biriyani. I went by around 3 and about to complete it by 3:45. But before finishing one of the server came and telling complete it soon and its closing time. I don't ever try this place again and not anymore :(

Author: Sreekanth Komaragiri
Description: Good food

Author: Sunil Subramanian
Description:
Hey,

My order was CB as they say (Chicken Biryani) With bone but i got aloo (potato) biryani there was no bone potato should not have bone... Thankz for tht and the person who took the call he says always OK Ok ok whether u complaint / compliments .. As a user small suggest maintain the consistency.

Author: Manohar Nandigam
Description: Been here once.. Good biryani and starters

Author: Sidhaarthan A
Description:
The same as the one at Koramangala. Not that great for Biriyani. We went in hoping that biriyani will be good.. You cant say it as Biriyani. It's some colored rice with gravy.
And never try anything in sea foods. The worst prawns i have ever had.
Only Pro: Chicken dry items are good!

Author: Shravan Somaiah
Description: Tried the meghana special biriyani and to be honest it was just like any other biriyani you get around..

Author: Ajay Kumar
Description: Though food may be good (since I haven't received it yet) the person or team who handles home delivery were really rude... Doesn't even know there is something called as Customer Experience". It's not just the food that matters, its the overall experience. They are PATHETIC. Will never ever ORDER AGAIN.

Author: Sujith G
Description: As we had a party in home, we went for a take away and ordered 2 packs of Chicken DUM biryani . Quality and taste are good with reasonable price. But If you love extra spicy food, this is not the right choice. And important one is this outlet is not matching the Koramangala outlet's taste. Koramangala outlet is better than Marathahalli. I will recommend this place.

Author: Vysak Anitha Shanmughan
Description: I just went Meghana alone for my dinner. The place was bit crowded on Tuesday evening.The attenders welcomed me in a very polite and good way. But me my  self find a place to sit on the 2nd floor.(I have been to Maghana even before also for a party from my cousin, i don't remember the items we ordered, but there was one fish item which i felt awesome taste). I ordered Meghana Special biriyani this time and it came with in 10 minute.The taste was awesome. One layer of rice and one layer of special chicken and gravy on top is how it looks. The taste was different from any other biriyanis i ever had. Simply love the spice and the biriyani. I can't tell that the ambiance is so good, because they made it like a normal hotel which is not having any good interior decorations or good music.

Author: Bon Vivant
Description:
I am a hardcore non vegetarian and Biryani is one of my favorite dish, Went to Mrghana for a Takeaway .
This one fairly new and well spaced.
Ordered for a Meghna Special Chk Biryani, Mutton one, a CHK Lolipop and famed Andhra Chill chicken (we were 3 and it was enough for us). It was good , i wont say its excellent . The salan they give its not at all tasty. Anyways overall it was value for money only if you are hungry......

Author: Shivam Singh
Description: Its been there(Marathalli) the whole time and i never noticed it! Untill we went for a movie in innovative and after the movie were looking for some place to have dinner. My boss suggested for this place as he said he knew they serve awesome biriyani. I was quite skeptical it but the moment our meghana sepcial biriyani arrived, i forgot everything and just dived in. It was awesome. Huge amount of chicken, wrapped in egg, good service, decent price. I loved it. Must try!

Author: Mohamed Ali Shajahan
Description:
Last night had been to the place for a treat. I did not know that this place was one of the famous casual dining chain of outlets in Bangalore. It was a Friday night, around 21:00hrs and to my surprise, the place was very much crowded and people were waiting to occupy the tables.This forced me to wonder about the specialty of the place. Fortunately one of my friend had arrived little earlier and occupied a table. The ambience was good with neatly laid out sofa seatings but the place looked clumsy & chaotic because of the crowd. We had the foll items:

Pepper Chicken - My friend had suggested this as it was his favorite. The meat was not crispy and was spongy. Usually the boneless chicken only contains well cleaned meet without skin or fat. To my shock the meet felt spongy because of the skin and fat. (Probably it was my bad luck.. not sure).
Meghana Spl Biryani - The first thing that you will notice about the dish is the greenish yellow colored chicken chunks sauteed in spices on top with the layered biryani on the bottom. Personally, the color was not appealing to me and moreover when i had the chicken, this also had the spongy feel.
Last Chicken Boneless Biryani: The taste was not up to the mark and i will guide you to better places for better biryani experience.
Service wise, the staffs are friendly and good.

BANG for BUCK - 2/10
Overall my experience with them was very bad and i am sure i will not recommend the place for any future occasions.

Author: Debabrata Misra
Description:
had lunch at the outlet today.
ambience-good
i tried andhra chicken ,egg masala,roomali roti,plain rice
the chicken was suparb,egg masala below average with a dominating masala gravy,roomali roti was pathetic,pre cooked not fresh....rice was below quality...so can visit there if nothing in arround and can have descent meal,but rate is very high vs the quantity and presentation

Author: Sandeep Ganga
Description:
after hearing that meghana foods was opened in marathahalli

sunday evening i went with my friends, byt the time it was fully packed and we got seats with in 5minutes...

we ordered tandoori chicken, chicken 555 and chicken boneless biriyani,

tandoori chicken and 555 are awesome and boneless biriyani was excellent as usual..

good value for money, best biriyani in marathahalli area

Author: Chandana Mahuli
Description:
After hearing a lot of hype about this place, finally got to try it .
The biryani was reasonably good,good value for money, but nothing like exceptional i would say. But better not try anything else here as the starters were pathetic.
Service was good and fast, even though the place was crowded.

Author: Joy
Description: My first experience with Meghana. Mind it! I am a hardcore Biryani person. Like eat, pray, sleep on Biryani. I guess it always pays to keep your expectations low. Coz boy, was I slam dunked. We ordered Chicken boneless biryani, fish biryani,egg biryani. While the meats in all the biryani were more than sufficient,succulent/juicy. The same can't be said about the taste. I mean, i am all for throat burning, stomach churning spicy but the kind of spicy Meghana churns out did not leave me craving for the biryani in spite of the spicy. Instead i thought i will make do with some cool raita. The spices i guess which they use is not your  red chilli powder or green chilies/red chilli. it must be the pepper, cardamom, cinnamon. They make you crave for water and not for the food. The meat was definitely good. You need to prepare yourself before heading out there.

Author: Rudrajyoti Biswas
Description:
A close to authentic hyderabadi biriyani is the verdict.

Argentina's second match was to start in an hour, so couldn't take a risk of dine in, and I dis take away.

Meghaha special biryani and chicken 555 were my choice.

To start with the starter, Chicken 555 tastes close to Chicken 65 of any Andhra style food joint. Little less spicy (read hot).

Meghana biryani was packed in an innovative way. Pieces of boneless chicken strips, rice were in 2 different packs. Chicken pieces taste far from any dum biriyani in Hyderabad. But, the rice taste near perfect. I hate salan and didnt bother to even taste it.

As with the restaurant, it was a nice clean place, and nothing extra ordinary to mention about ambience. Oh, one thing was of course noticeable, that on the tables, food was not served on plates, banana leaves instead.

So, in all, it would be a nice experience for a budget biriyani dinner.

Author: Ramesh B
Description:
Went for a Takeaway after having had a good experience at the Residency road Branch.
This one fairly new and well spaced. Walked in on Saturday around 9:30 PM and both floors full with people having thier favorites

Ordered for a Meghna Special Chk Biryani, Mutton one, a CHK Lolipop and famed Andhra Chill chicken.

Pros
All items fairly decent and nothing outstanding . Portions are adequate and could have been more for the money.
Quick take away service and well packed.

Cons:
No standout and nothing hyderabadi or Andhra with respect to falvours.
Almost all the mutton pieces had too much of slat + bone and with a exception of a solo one everything has lot of dry fat!.

Will i go back - May be 6 months down the line to check if there is an improvement i can see/hear
Our Blooper was the chicken for Special Biryani was packed separately and we did not relaise it and was wondering the special means with meat!.

Author: Priyanka Nagpal
Description:
I have heard a lot about this outlet in Koramangala and since they have opened a branch in Marathahalli. I thought of giving it a try.
Though I am not a biryani fan but my husband really loved the biryani he had there for office lunch outing. I was in mood for a biryani so we ordered for a home delivery.
We ordered
Andhra chilli chicken
Boneless chicken biryani
Meghna special biryani
Ok... the chilli chicken was really a CHILLI Chicken. It was made out of green chilli gravy (see the pic)... oh it was damn spicy and my hubby was happy to eat it as he love extra spicy food.
The chicken biryani was nice as well as the special biryani. And again it was spicy... also the quantity was quite good.
This place goes by the tag of being Andhra style and it proves it.
I will recommend this place if you really want something spicy to eat.
Ambience : kind of a local mess
Food : 3/5
Service : 4/5 ( we live on 11th floor - somehow our block lift was not working so in confusion he climbed the stairs.)

Author: Nandita Bayan
Description: Have been hearing about this place from quite sometime. Finally made it. Been here for Friday afternoon lunch. We were 4 of us. Too much crowded, had to wait for our table. We ordered mushroom manchurian, egg biriyani, lolipop biriyani and boneless chicken biriyani with coke and fanta. It was an overdose of chicken. Food was average but a bit spicy and not very authentic taste of biriyani. Rice quality was good, stuff behavior ok and ambiance - dont expect much.

Author: Hrudaya Prasanna Dash
Description:
As now marathalli is becoming a hub for Biryanis lover, Meghana Foods and Hyderabad Biryaani adds to the list.
Both opened nearly at the same time and running successfully.

I visited Meghana Foods last weekand and the biryani's were upto the expectation.It may be too spicy for somebody but the flavours and rice quality was goood.
The Chicken lollypops were tasty and the mutton biryani was also nice with tender mutton pieces .

Service was Ok.
Ambience nothing to hope much as it gets crowded very quickly.
Recommended for Biryani Lovers.

Author: Subasrilekha Srinivas
Description: Had been to their other outlets and loved their food and service. Likewise, this outlet also didn't disappoint us in any mean. Their biriyani will be always my favourite and the service is totally outstanding. This time I tried their mutton pepper fry and trust me it was lipsmacking.

Author: Ranjith Arthur Coapullai
Description:
Managed to go here on a Sunday afternoon at around 2:00PM and man this place was a total mess.
I have been eating at Meghana since 2007 and this place disappointed me.
There were around 50-100 people waiting everywhere like Roaches either to get a table or to get a parcel.
It was madness. I left the place and came back at 3:30 to avoid the crowd.

Food:
Disappointed me. It did not taste like the Biriyani in Koramangala, Brigade Road or Jayanagar. The biriyani lacked flavour and was dry. The major letdown is that the size of the pieces in the Boneless biriyani were much smaller than the ones elsewhere and the price was 10 rupees higher than Koramangala (I might be wrong).

Another let down is that the floors were dirty and slippery, u can see people mopping the floor while ur eating.
Typical roadside experience.

Note to Management: My negative review is not going to affect your business in any way because you generate such high volumes and losing one customer wont affect you. I will never come to Marthahalli again. I prefer the other 3 branches.

Also, please move your take away counter somewhere else and the restaurant will transform magically.

Author: Purna
Description:
After too many recommendations from my friends in kormangala, I was happy that meghana was going to open its restaurant in marathahalli. Expectations were too high when I went there for the first time but Well, I should say my they didn't disappoint me.
Though I can not say that the biryani here is the best, but starters definitely are awesome. Don't miss apolo fish, chicken lollipop and lemon chicken here. In summary, ambience is ok ( a bit noisy) and Food is really good (May be a bit spicy for my friends from north India).
It's not even couple of months that meghana opened its shop here and this is already my third visit. My recent visit was with 15 people, and it costed 5500 approx so it isn't that expensive either. Every time we visited here, we made sure we are there before 12:30 for lunch and by 8pm for diner because, as is with any other successful restaurant, waiting time here can be grueling. I have friends who waited to get a seat for over an hour on weekends but have to finally give up and visit nandhana or biryani zone nearby.

Author: IGobbler
Description:
Visited the restaurant this weekend. My wife suggested this place as she heard good about it from her colleagues. We had Chicken Biriyani and Khundur (excuse me if I spelt it wrong) Chicken as the Starter. The Biriyani and the Starter was very delicious, but bit spicy for us as we always have less/medium spicy food at home/hotels. It should be ok for those who are fond of Hyderabadi delicacies. We also happened to hear the people sitting opposite to us complaining about the spiciness of some other type of Biriyani they ordered (it was really looking spicy and tasty too :)))

We went there on a Friday afternoon and the place was so crowded. But the staffs were taking care of the customers well in finding a place quickly.

Ambience - 3.5/5
Food - 4/5
Price - 4/5
Customer Service - 4.5/5

Author: Nanditha Gopi
Description:
Finally I can cross this off my list - and I can say I've been to all the outlets in Bangalore!
We went here for our team dinner - 11 people. So we had to wait a while for a table - but considering the rush - we didn't have to wait all that much.
They pushed tables together and got us a place.
And we had a mix of vegetarians and non-vegetarians.
So we ordered both.
We had the special for the day - which you must never say NO to.
And Rotis and Chicken Kalimirch and Chicken Kolhapuri - spicy and yum.
Then the Biriyani - loaded with chicken as usual and really good :)
Nobody was disappointed :)

Author: Chander
Description:
Brilliant starter, salty Biryani.
They served huge portions, couldn't finish more than two biryani among three of us (sigh, used to finish one large pizza in one sitting few years back). Ordered green chicken as appetizer - was brilliant, for lack of words. The boneless chicken in their special biryani was great as well. The pieces in the normal biryani however were very salty though. Will definitely give it a try again. I am more inclined to get a home delivery to dine in though. They take a lot of time to serve biryani (Biryani zone is more efficient in this aspect)

Author: Prateek Jairath
Description: This outlet is not at all matching up the standards of kormangala one...!!! Biryani's were really bad...

Author: Matthew Thomas
Description:
Its a good place to try a biriyani. The preparation is a bit different than usual biriyanis.. I wished to have a prawns biriyani but the lunch was about closing at 4pm, only chicken was available and it was good..
The chicken quantity is a little less and I think over a period of time, u may not like the taste..
Overall recommend this place to be checked out.

Author: Siddharth Sinha
Description:
Pathetic Biryani. Only Black Savages can eat this red rice full of red-chilly.

In South India, people do not know what is "Spicy" .. In reality it means different types of masala like cooriander, Asafoetida,Red pepper,Peppercorns,Cummins, Capers, Bay Leaf, Cinnamon, cardamom etc. However they have skipped all this and add red-chilly 200g in anything you say spicy, taste is secondary to them.

Utterly disappointing and i left the full plate being unable to torture my stomach with this pathetic biryani. It sucks to say the least. In the name of biryani, volcanic red chilly is served with rice here !!

Author: Sheldon Rodrigues
Description:
A craving for biryani and i ended up at Meghana Foods last night . As everyone says it's biryani is undoubtedly the best in town . The succulent pieces of chicken just make your mouth water . To add to it we also had starters Meghana's chicken 555 which was good . However the Apollo fish starter was not up to the mark .
Overall bill 800 for 3 of us totally worth it .
So next time if you are craving for some good biryani and want value for money just drop in here to be amazed !!

Author: Aakash Dhar
Description: in less than 20  words"lacked the punch of the Andhra style biryani " recently visited this place with my sister as she said it served one of the best biryani well when we reached there the place was really crowded i guess we selected a wrong time to visit there it was afternoon and i was starving so anyhow we went in as i said the place was crowded luckily we got ourselves a place to sit we ordered the meghna special biryani it took more than 25 mins to arrive and even wen it did they didn't bring us the raita and the masala which they give with the biryani we brought this in notice of the waiters but he was too busy didnt even bother to listen on the second shot wen he did listen he said would get it but he still didnt so anyways till now we were extremely hungry so we just went without it coming to "FOOD" i am a ardent worshiper of biryani and u really have high hopes with a Andhra style eatery to have the  best biryani but it fell short in my expectation it lacked the punch of Andhra biryani the only thng special abt the biryani was a layer of coating of egg and well that's it nothing more. ambiance of the place is "good" but the wait time is too damn high if u plan to go during the rush hour keep an hour handy

Author: Venkatesh Hegde
Description: I'm a veggie. That automatically means I should not have too many expectations at a Biryani house. Since Meghana foods is well known, I thought Ok, there's a tiny chance I might be wrong. Sadly, that wasn't the case. The veg dum biryani is very average. The rice wasn't even cooked well. Not worth a second visit, for a veggie.

Author: Bharat S
Description: Seriously disappointing experience every time I visit or get it packed. I found chicken pieces in Veg. Biriyani few times and the last time I went to get it packed, the fellows there refused to pack extra masala, as I like eating it with added spice. Biryani was just like colored rice with no taste!!. I never had such a bad experience in any of the other branches of Meghana...

Author: Suhas Rajnalkar
Description:
A good restaurant either for having the food leisurely or when you're in hurry. This restaurant is all worthy for the value you pay. They serve a good range of biriyani varities and starters which are typically andhra cuisine. We had several kinds but we like mutton biriyani and the red chilli chicken. You need to wait for a couple minutes before you get settled here.

Food : 4/5
Service : 4/5
Ambience : 3.5/5
Value for money : 4/5

Author: Rahul
Description:
Added on October 19 - chicken rayalaseema is a must try!!

Review

After a rather long wait, went to Meghana Foods' Marathahalli outlet. Being a regular at the residency road one, I was very keenly looking forward to this once, since it is very close to my home.

Since it was a sunday, we went rather early knowing the crowd here. We got a table without any waiting. The ambiance is very similar to residency road outlet with similar seating. Nothing fancy about the looks of the place, just good enough to be called a good restaurant.

After skipping the breakfast, I had no doubt on what to eat. It was Andhra meals time. We were 4 and we ordered Gobi manchurian and meghana special chicken 555 for starters. 3 of us went with meals while one with chicken biriyani. Gobi manchurian is a tried and tested starter and it din't disappoint us. Chicken 555 was a slight disappointment. The taste was not as brilliant as was suggested the waiter. It was good no doubt, but missed the wow factor. Quantity wise it was pretty good. Soon the meals came and I was feasting on rice, pappu (dal), cabbage curry and gongura pickle. I like the gongura picke more at Nandana Palace than here, but nevertheless the combination along with rice and pappu is deadly!!

After couple of rounds of mountain loads of rice, I was in position where I could hardly move. Thats what Andhra meals does to people!! The service level was good and responsive. The damage for the lunch for 4 of us came to ~950 which is definitely not bad. I would rate this .5 less than residency road, since I found the pappu there to be more tastier!!

Meghana is one of the best Andhra restaurants in Bangalore without doubt. I shall definitely keep going back to this place whenever I have that urge for Andhra food!!

Author: Venkatesh Abburi
Description:
This is one Andhra restaurant that has been running successfully since years, that is what i heard about it. Seems they have few more branches in the city.

Nothing special to say about the ambience. It is just regular. It s too crowded. Thank God i was comforted with a table with out waiting for a long time.

They are really bad at service. The stewart does nothing but shouting at the subordinates. I was in a rush so i didn't pay much attention to it.

We ordered Meghana Special Chicken Biryani, Paneer Biryani & Veg Thali. Chicken in the biryani was awesome but the rice lacks the yum that Hyderabadi Biryani generally does have. Coming to the Paneer Biryani, it is a feast for veggies. Well fried Paneer with the same kind of rice which lacks the real yum of Biryani. But the Veg Thali was really good, they even serve the famous Andhra food Ulavacharu.

Ambience: 3/5
Service: 2/5
Food: 3.5/5

Author: Popo Popo
Description:
I am a big fan of biriyani, and have eaten at all the places around Marathahalli that serves biriyani. I have heard a lot about the Koramangla branch, and Meghana's special biriyani , chicken coated with egg, which I think is too fatty for my liking. I thought of trying the newly opened Meghana foods in Marathahalli, next to Staples. So, on a Friday evening, I dropped by to get a biriyani packed. As i was waiting, I could see the place was almost half full by 7 pm. I waited for 15 mins, finally I got my chicken biriyani (Rs 170).

Coming to the biriyani, it was pretty average. It was not hot at all, with every bite you get pieces of cinnamon and cloves in your mouth, that literally burns your mouth. The garam masala was overpowering. The rice had some kind of a smell that comes from old rice. I was really not happy eating this average biriyani.

Author: Varsha Nadig
Description:
To begin with, this review is specific to our experience at Marathahalli branch of Meghana Foods. Our multiple experiences at their Jayanagar branch were good.

Our experiece this time was surely a Friday evening spoiler. The starters we had ordered were either not available or were cold and overcooked. We requested the manager to prepare the starter again and the second time it was better, though still coldish. All the three main things that matter in a restaurant - table, plates and spoons - were dirty. Waiters were stinking and they definitely needed training on how to serve food. At the end of our meal, we requested the waiter to pack the remaining food and on his way out he spilt close to half of the remaining starter on me (so my food bill = food bill + dry cleaning bill).

Another area of improvement is the waiting lounge. They do not have a proper area where customers can wait so they end up waiting next to your table while you are eating. This is a bit uncomfortable as there are usually a big number of people who are waiting.

Last and this might be specific to me. I do not trust places which do not expect me to sign on the merchant's copy of the credit card receipt. I had to insist that I want to see the receipt and sign.

Overall, a poor experience and to repeat spoilt our Friday evening.

Author: Doctor Foodie
Description:
well haters will be haters .
i have heard many people say it i not exactly a briyani but for me its one of the best briyani in town.
qualifies for me if it taste good.
Many a times we have ordered family packs for 12 and 13 people.
It is boneless and u have to add dry chicken on top. So it differs from traditional briyani.

Author: Sriram Iyer
Description:
When their Koramangala outlet opened a few years ago, I was already bored of eating the Hyderabadi and pseudo-Hyderabad variety of Biryani served across Bangalore. But this was very close to my office then, so went ahead and tried it. The Andhra style boneless chicken biryani served here has been my faourite ever since. Not been there for a couple of years since I moved to ORR. To my utter delight, they have opened a branch closer home. Same quality, better ambience and somewhat more hygienic (hope so!).
The only two items that I order always are Andhra style boneless chicken biryani and Nellore chicken dry. Always delicious and done to perfection.
This is a no frills restaurant, somewhat similar to the Shanti Sagars of Bangalore in ambience, decor, service quality etc., so don't expect anything more than finger licking, eyes watering and throat burning spicy biryani.

Author: Vignesh KR
Description:
After a long fun filled day at wonderla we reached Marathahalli at around 9:30PM . The day was filled with many a breaks for short eats and we were too tired for a full fledged dinner but yet knew that if we skipped it we would be hungry in the middle of the night . We all voted between take away and dine in and dine in triumphed . The 6 of us ( 3 boys and 3 girls ) parked our car on the service lane opposite to the recently opened Meghna biriyani in Marathahalli and headed for the same . The exterior look of the restaurant was very plain and cheaply done and we would have never stepped into any other restaurant with similar looks but this was Meghna Biriyani .... It was a Biriyani connoisseurs haven and long been on the wish list of me and a couple of others in our group .... The first floor adorned by its mediocre interiors ( but much better than the exterior ) was packed as expected and had a lot of people waiting for a table ... We carried on to the second floor and found a nice comfortable seat on the corner for our group ... It was a bit cold but the service boy obliged to our request to switch off the fan right above us .... The menu was that of any typical andhra restaurant with usual dishes like Guntoor
chicken , Apollo fish , andhra style chili chicken etc but we went straight for the biriyanis ....we ordered an 'Meghna special biriyani' and a 'boneless chicken biriyani' , one veg meals along with few soft drinks and butter milk . The biriyanis were quick to arrive and were out of this world .Meghna special was a Perfectly cooked hyderabadi biriyani with the right level of spices topped with tender chicken that had been marinated and cooked in a special curd based marinade . The boneless chicken biriyani was very similar but was topped with small red colored chicken kebab like pieces which bought about a nice variation in taste .... But the highlight was the mirchi ka Salan which was perfectly spiced and of the proper consistency ... The union of the biriyani with the Salan was fantastic and addictive leaving you craving for more with each bite ... The raitha was good too .... The veg meals was pretty decent ...The bill came close to around 600 ... The butter milk and soft drinks never reached us and we cancelled the same .... All in all we left very contended and were waiting to reach home and crash .... Will definitely visit this place again and would recommend the same to anyone who wants to try some nice hyderabadi biriyani at a reasonable price ...

Author: Sudheer Kopparapu
Description: Biryani is too good. Service was little slow as it was crowded on friday afternoon but still OK. Reasonable prices.

Author: Roshan
Description:
Heard about this place this that they serve the best Biriyani in Banaglore, So visited the the new marathalli counter.
The concept of banana leaves was different but not appropriate as it should be used with pure veg foods or in temples not in biriyani counters.

Ordered a Chicken Biriyani and a Prawn Biriyani, Chicken Biriyani taste was fine but not the best in Bangalore a bit disappointed as chicken quantity was very less, The next was Prawn Biriyani and it was the WORST Prawn Biriyani I have ever had, prawns were totally dry and can assure you it was not fresh when asked the waiter they said that they server it like that only so indirectly he was saying that they don't server fresh prawns.

Once again NEVER EVER order Prawns Biriyani here.

Author: Jawahar Babu
Description: its good

Author: Naveen Ravichandren
Description:
As Usual, Simply Spicy and Awesome Chicken Boneless Biryani!!!
Love you Meghana Foods Since 2008!!!

Author: Kiran Kumar
Description:
I'm a great fan of dum biryani...Marathahalli outlet is near to my office, so i visited this outlet today with hope that i will taste a great biryani today (after looking at reviews of koramangala and other outlets)....This outlet didn't reach to the taste that other outlets serve....We ordered "egg biryani", garlic was overly used and giving a very bad flavor to biryani...not a great tasting biryani...disappointed :(...I hope marathalli outlet management sees my comment and engage with other outlets to improve taste...you're just newbie in this area, get a "best first impression", otherwise, you will loose the ground...(RE-RATED comments: absolutely worthless food and service...people should stop encouraging such restaurants...)

Food: 3/5 (Re-rated 1/5)
Ambience: 4/5 (floor was not clean and slippery)
Service: 4/5 (except the coordination was lacking among service persons) (Re-rated 1/5 - absolute negligence)
Price: 3/5 (overpriced for the Taste)  (Re-rated 1/5; money wasted on tasteless Meghana special biryani and chilly chicken )

Overall: I would rather go to below ORR outlets for tasty (4/5) biryani .
-> Hyderabad Biryani House, Old airport road
-> Mani's, Bellandur (not any other outlets of Mani's)
-> Nizam's Kitchen, Marathahalli
-> Cauvery biryani, near cisco, ORR (less price, tastes better than average)
-> Sahithi Restaurant, near kadubeesanahalli junction; ORR;  (less price, tastes better than average;;fried chicken biryani)

Author: Amod Shankar
Description:
So here my wish comes true when these guys open outlet in marathahalli. About the wish, I have had a plan to visit the best outlets in bangalore which serve a particular cuisine. About chicken biriyani, I had read about meghana foods, koramangala and had plans to visit it, but residing in marathahalli, I was not getting time to visit there.

So they opened on a friday and we went there 2 days later, for dinner on sunday.

Since it is a new outlet, superbly clean tables and everything maintained nicely were noticable. The place had a decent crowd but we found a table easily.

Coming to the order, we ordered meghana special chicken biriyani(182 Rs per plate) and coke since we didnot want to take any chances. They serve the food on banana leafs and it was nice and clean.

When the food came, By the looks of it, I was sure my hunt for good biriyani in bangalore has come to an end.
And boy ! The biriyani was out of this world. Superbly cooked rice, amazing(and lots of) boneless chicken pieces and the aroma was mesmerizing. The biriyani outclassed each and every restaurant in bangalore which I have had biriyani into. The raita and complimentary gravy was also superb. We had a hard time finishing the biriyani since the amount was more than enough.

I would recommend this restaurant to all those who are looking(and have not visited this place) for top-class biriyani.

There were a few mosquitoes though, which irritated us a bit.

However, this places is seriously RECOMMENDED ! for biriyani.

Author: Gaurav Pradhan
Description:
Tried out the new outlet in Marathalli and I ordered the usual - Boneless Chicken Biryani and Non-Veg starter. I decided to have it home delivered. It took about 45 mins for the food to be delivered but it was worth the wait. The quality and the packaging of the food is just like any other outlet, simply amazing!

Cons
- They don't have cold drinks that can be delivered
- They staff is a bit rude - my first order was for Rs. 337 and they were not ready to deliver the same order the second time because it was not above Rs.350 (which it would have been, if they had cold drinks).

Anyways, I will still stick with my taste buds and continue to order with a hope that something is done about the staff.

Author: Gayathri Prabhu
Description: We tried out the new outlet at Marathahalli today. We opted for  takeaway.  The veg biriyani and gobi starter  were quite good. The biriyani was perfectly spiced and cooked just right. Ambience of the place is quite nice too and it was a full house just a week after opening.

Author: Paurush Singh
Description:
Food : 4.5/5 One of the best Biryani in town.
